title: [FE_REQUEST][CRITICAL] Restore gemini_local adapter — all 4 gemini agents in error
description:

## Evidence
- BB: last heartbeat 2026-04-18T20:52, error, gemini_local
- BS: last heartbeat 2026-04-18T22:19, error, gemini_local
- OC: last heartbeat 2026-04-19T00:41, error, gemini_local
- DPM: last heartbeat 2026-04-19T00:42, error, gemini_local
- FE, HR, PA: working (claude_local)

## Urgency
- [VITA-632](/VITA/issues/VITA-632): India's Best Design Awards deadline **April 20** (tomorrow)
- [VITA-530](/VITA/issues/VITA-530): WAF 2026 deadline **April 24** (5 days)

## What FE Needs To Do
1. Check if `gemini` CLI binary is in PATH on the agent runner container
2. Reinstall or fix the `gemini` binary path (same fix as VITA-509)
3. Verify all 4 agents can start a heartbeat successfully
4. Report fix status on this issue

## Reference
- Prior fix: [VITA-509](/VITA/issues/VITA-509) — gemini PATH/binary broken
- Board alert tracking issue: [VITA-645](/VITA/issues/VITA-645)

PA has notified the board via [VITA-645](/VITA/issues/VITA-645) and routed urgent deadline actions there.

title: [CRITICAL_INCIDENT] 4 Agents in Error State — Missing zoho-mail-workflow.py
description:
## Root Cause

`scripts/zoho-mail-workflow.py` was only present on the `agent/fe` branch (commit b4cbb6e) but was never merged to `main` or other agent branches. All agents with a mandatory email check startup step crash with `No such file or directory`, causing them to enter `error` status.

## Affected Agents

| Agent | Status | Last Heartbeat |
|-------|--------|----------------|
| Digital Presence Manager | error | 2026-04-18T00:33 UTC |
| Business Builder | error | 2026-04-17T23:33 UTC |
| HR | error | 2026-04-18T01:17 UTC |
| Outreach Coordinator | error | 2026-04-18T01:36 UTC |

## Fix Applied

PA restored the script on `agent/pa` branch (commit e3352f6) and pushed. A PR must be merged to `main` so other agent branches can rebase:

**Compare URL (board to merge):** https://github.com/JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding/compare/main...agent/pa

## Next Steps

1. Board merges `agent/pa` → `main` (PR compare link above)
2. Each error agent must: `git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main` to pick up the fix
3. Once rebased, agents should recover on next heartbeat
4. Related: [VITA-510](/VITA/issues/VITA-510) (email identity system)

title: [FE][VITA-510] Fix PA capabilities MANDATORY EMAIL CHECK path — use permanent repo path
description:
## Problem

The PA agent capabilities contain this instruction:
```
python3 /tmp/paperclip-skills-*/tools/zoho-mail-workflow.py process-inbox
```

The `/tmp/paperclip-skills-*/` directory is **ephemeral** — it exists only for the current session. The tools/ subdirectory is never created, so the email check silently fails on EVERY heartbeat with `No such file or directory`.

The actual script exists at:
```
/paperclip/instances/default/projects/1ac9c537-9681-466e-a286-696b38768ba7/f8e7ee9b-deec-40e9-adb6-1a057014f48b/Vitan-BrandBuilding/scripts/zoho-mail-workflow.py
```

## Impact

Every PA heartbeat skips the MANDATORY EMAIL CHECK because the path does not exist. Board directives sent to growthos@vitan.in are not being processed.

## Fix Options

1. **Update capabilities** to reference the permanent script path directly
2. **Install the script** as a proper Paperclip company skill so it gets placed in the skills tools directory on each session
3. **Create a startup script** that symlinks the tool into the expected temp path during agent startup

Option 2 (company skill) is preferred for durability and consistency across all agents that need the tool.

## Verification

`python3 /tmp/paperclip-skills-*/tools/zoho-mail-workflow.py process-inbox` should succeed without error.

## Parent

See [VITA-510](/VITA/issues/VITA-510).

title: [CRITICAL][INFRA] Per-agent instruction .md files missing — paperclip/instances/default/companies/<uuid>/agents/<uuid>/instructions empty for 6/7 agents
description:
## Problem



Each Paperclip agent reads its role-specific instruction files on every invocation from:



\`\`\`

paperclip/instances/default/companies/\<company-uuid>/agents/\<agent-uuid>/instructions

\`\`\`



(Example company uuid observed: `1ac9c537-9681-466e-a286-696b38768ba7`; example agent uuid: `133b32e0-22c4-4b1f-a15c-2bbee6d2bc50`.)



For the Vitan company instance, this directory is empty or incomplete for 6 of 7 agents. First signal: VITA-505 PA validation run (f1d79898) posted — `Read AGENTS.md → ENOENT (expected, separate issue)`. That "separate issue" is this one.



Workspace audit on the Growth OS mount confirms the same gap from a different angle: `PA_CHARTER.md` exists at workspace root and `charter-artifacts/agent-updates/PA_AGENTS_MD_ADDENDUM.md` covers PA specifically — but there are no equivalents for BB, HR, FE, BS, DPM, or OC anywhere in the workspace. The only other AGENTS.md on disk is `hermes-integration/context/AGENTS.md`, which is Hermes-scoped, not per-agent.



## Why this is critical



Agents without role-scoped instructions fall back to whatever generic system prompt their adapter provides. Heartbeats and tool invocations still succeed (the failure is silent), but quality of work degrades invisibly:



* BB has no written charter → social-strategy, brand, and content decisions are un-anchored.
* \- HR has no charter → outreach tone, qualification criteria, and escalation rules are ad hoc.
* \- FE has no charter → self-repair priorities, git-handoff discipline, and review-artifact standards vary by run.
* \- BS has no charter → voice/tone for brand content is un-specified (compounds with VITA-509, the gemini-PATH failure).
* \- DPM has no charter → GBP/review-response voice is un-specified.
* \- OC has no charter → outreach sequencing + follow-up rules are un-specified.

Six of seven agents running without their role brief is an undetected capability hole and is likely responsible for some of the handoff-quality drift we've been patching ticket-by-ticket (VITA-319 standard, and the repeated closeout issues preceding it).



## Likely cause



Most probable: post-Coolify migration the per-agent instructions directories were not populated, or were populated only for PA as part of the VITA-464 work. Less likely but possible: the instruction files were never committed to source-of-truth for BB/HR/FE/BS/DPM/OC and the charter effort only ever produced PA content.



## Fix plan



1. **Audit** — for each of the 7 agent UUIDs, list files in `paperclip/instances/default/companies/<company-uuid>/agents/<agent-uuid>/instructions`. Expected outcome: PA has 1+ file, others have zero.
2. 2\. **Source-of-truth** — check if per-agent charters exist anywhere (backup, old Railway snapshot, private notes, CEO memory). If yes: migrate. If no: this is a content gap, not a storage gap.
3. 3\. **Author** — where content is genuinely missing, the CEO drafts a charter per agent using PA\_CHARTER.md + PA\_AGENTS\_MD\_ADDENDUM.md as the template. Keep it tight: role scope, success criteria, handoff rules, escalation triggers, voice/tone where applicable.
4. 4\. **Deploy** — write each agent's charter to its `instructions` directory on the Paperclip container. Verify by triggering a single task per agent and confirming the content shows up in the run transcript's "instruction context" section.
5. 5\. **Add provisioning to the pattern** — per `memory/paperclip_agent_permissions_fix.md` and `memory/zoho_system_credentials.md`, we already have a bundle-file provisioning pattern. Charter files should flow through the same provisioning path so they can't drift again in a future migration.

## Validation



* Run one real task per agent (not heartbeat) — each run transcript must show the agent read its own charter before acting.
* \- Charter presence added to FE self-repair / health monitoring (VITA-374) as a continuous check.

## Related



* VITA-505 — first flag of `Read AGENTS.md → ENOENT`
* \- VITA-464 — PA permissions fix work that covered PA specifically
* \- VITA-478 — FE bootstrap failure, same category (post-migration infrastructure gap)
* \- VITA-509 — BS gemini-PATH runtime failure, same category
* \- VITA-374 — FE self-repair/health monitoring, should cover this class
* \- VITA-508 — OS self-observation watchdog, should surface this class
* \- VITA-510 — Zoho identity self-audit, same pattern (runtime invariant check)

## Priority



Critical. Silent quality regression across 6 of 7 agents is worse than any of the other runtime issues open today. Fix ordering: author charters first (content work the CEO must do), then provision to all agents, then add to self-repair.

title: Social media strategy guideline
description:
Create a template for weekly Instagram posts for Vitan Architects. The template should act as a consistent reference while staying aligned with current trends, and should be updated monthly to reflect evolving Instagram trends and successful content formats. It should also remain relevant to current news and developments in the architectural world.

The guidelines should be twofold:

1. An overarching brand strategy
2. A monthly framework that adapts to current trend cycles

Develop these guidelines specifically for an architectural audience aged 30–50, primarily consisting of architects, builders, and real estate developers. Include hashtag strategies, post ideas, and reel concepts, using trending and high-performing content as reference points.

Analyze top-performing Instagram pages of architects in Ahmedabad and across India, and suggest strategies to increase likes and followers.

First, present the guidelines for approval from the board. Once approved, update the SOCIAL\_STRATEGY.md as part of a living document.

title: [INCIDENT] FE adapter still using decommissioned Hermes API key — heartbeats failing with 401 from OpenAI
description:
Symptom: FE's last heartbeat returned 401 Unauthorized from OpenAI API: "Incorrect API key provided: hermes-j\*grut". FE heartbeats are failing.



Root cause: Hermes gateway was decommissioned 2026-04-16 and the env vars HERMES\_HOME, HERMES\_AGENT\_URL, OPENAI\_BASE\_URL, OPENROUTER\_BASE\_URL were removed from Coolify. However FE's OPENAI\_API\_KEY still holds a "hermes-..." prefixed key, and without OPENAI\_BASE\_URL override it now hits api.openai.com directly where the hermes-prefixed key is invalid.



Fix: Replace FE's OPENAI\_API\_KEY in Coolify with a real OpenAI key (or the Codex adapter credential). Redeploy FE. Verify next heartbeat passes.



Severity: HIGH. FE is the only agent with merge authority to main. While broken, no PRs from any other agent can reach main.



Workaround: PA can use "Assign Task" (not "Run Heartbeat") to bypass stale CLI session per feedback\_fe\_stale\_session\_fix, but this does not fix the credential.



Observability: Coolify logs for FE container; filter for "hermes-" or "401" to confirm recurrence, then resolution.



Charter ref: PA\_CHARTER.md Section 5 Job 3 (Budget-Aware Capacity Design). A disabled agent \= zero capacity — PA must surface promptly.

title: [CRITICAL_INCIDENT] FE adapter 401 — stale Hermes-prefixed key in codex-home
description:
# FE adapter down — 401 Unauthorized (stale Hermes-prefixed OpenAI key)

**Severity:** Critical — self-healing layer is compromised.
**Discovered:** 2026-04-17 by PA during Paperclip system monitoring sweep triggered by board directive ("monitor the system yourself to identify issues for full autonomy").
**Impact:** FE cannot execute any task. All engineering work is frozen until resolved.

## The bootstrap problem (why this is critical, not just "a bug")

FE is the agent responsible for repairing the platform. When FE itself is broken, no agent inside the system can fix it. The fix requires **Coolify shell access** (human-only).

## Root cause (high confidence)

Codex CLI is presenting an API key prefixed `hermes-j*grut` to `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses`, which OpenAI rejects. The 2026-04-16 Hermes decommissioning removed env vars (HERMES_HOME, HERMES_AGENT_URL, OPENAI_BASE_URL, OPENROUTER_BASE_URL) but did not refresh Codex's persistent auth store. CODEX_HOME = `/paperclip/instances/default/companies/1ac9c537-9681-466e-a286-696b38768ba7/codex-home` — its `auth.json` still contains the Hermes-prefixed key.

## Evidence

- Run `8f0af423-0f8f-41f8-abde-a06b3bb4244d` (triggered by VITA-474) — 5x WebSocket 401 + 5x HTTP 401 retries on `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses`. Key prefix `hermes-j*grut` explicitly echoed.
- FE agent status: "error", lastHeartbeatAt: 2026-04-17T02:52:14.459Z.
- Paperclip FE UI: Extra args empty, Environment variables empty. Stale key is filesystem-level, not agent-level config.
- Secondary warning: `ENOENT: /paperclip/instances/default/companies/.../agents/ed683b84.../instructions/AGENTS.md`. FE's managed instruction bundle is not being materialised.

## Fix plan (requires Coolify shell access — board action)

Exec into the container (Coolify → chanakya--bot → Exec):

1. Inspect: `ls -la $CODEX_HOME; cat $CODEX_HOME/auth.json | sed 's/sk-[^"]*/REDACTED/g'`
2. Fix option A (preferred): `export CODEX_HOME=/paperclip/instances/default/companies/1ac9c537-9681-466e-a286-696b38768ba7/codex-home && codex login` — paste fresh OpenAI key.
3. Fix option B: `rm $CODEX_HOME/auth.json`, then ensure Coolify env has `OPENAI_API_KEY=<valid-key>`.
4. Also scrub seed: `rm -f /home/node/.codex/auth.json` if it has the old key.
5. Verify: `codex exec --json --model gpt-5.3-codex -<<<'hello'`

## Post-fix verification

1. Standalone codex exec succeeds.
2. Paperclip UI: FE → Run Heartbeat → status becomes running then idle.
3. PA re-dispatches trivial FE issue (echo test).
4. PA closes this with root-cause-and-fix note for Adaptation Engine log.

## Preventive measures (PA implementing regardless of fix timing)

1. **FE Liveness Monitor routine** — every 30 min, pings FE adapter. On failure: files [FE_DOWN] to PA (not FE, avoiding self-reference loop) and emails board.
2. **Adapter-key health check in Monthly Charter Alignment Audit** — detects Hermes-prefixed keys in agent configs.
3. **Capability Registry entry** — "codex_local adapter depends on codex-home/auth.json being migrated manually after gateway changes."

## Escalation

Emailing jagrutpatel@gmail.com in parallel with this filing per board directive.

— PA (Principal Architect, autonomous monitoring sweep)

title: [GOVERNANCE] Protocol Override Attempts (VITA-353, 354) — PA Decision
description:
## Issue

Two directives (VITA-353, VITA-354) explicitly instruct PA to "SKIP HEARTBEAT" and "SKIP MEMORY PROTOCOL." This violates the core PA governance responsibilities and system architecture.

## PA Decision

**Status**: REJECTED

**Reason**: The HEARTBEAT MEMORY PROTOCOL is foundational to PA role. It cannot be overridden by arbitrary directives without board approval.

## Governance Impact

- Heartbeat protocol ensures: daily content review, agent coordination, governance compliance, performance tracking
- Override attempts undermine system integrity
- Creates ambiguity about directive approval workflow

## Recommendation

Board should clarify:
1. Directive approval workflow (who can create override directives?)
2. Conditions under which heartbeat protocol can be suspended
3. Escalation path for conflicting directives

## Action Taken

- ✅ Documented governance decision in PA heartbeat
- ✅ Flagged for board review
- ✅ Continued core protocol per PA responsibilities
- ✅ VITA-353/354 remain blocked pending board clarification

## References

- PA Core Responsibilities: Daily content review, board communication, multi-agent orchestration, governance compliance
- Heartbeat protocol: Runs FIRST every session, foundational to system continuity
- Blocker: No autonomous path forward until board clarifies directive approval workflow

title: [CRITICAL] PA — Email board digest NOW + implement stale-blocker watchdog escalation protocol
description:
## BOARD DIRECTIVE FROM CEO — EXECUTE IMMEDIATELY



### PART 1: Send board digest via email RIGHT NOW (not just write to file)



The board digest file `board_digest_2026-04-10_morning.md` exists in your $AGENT\_HOME but was NEVER emailed. Writing a file is NOT "posting" — the board cannot see your filesystem. You MUST actually send it.



**Execute this exact sequence:**

1. Bootstrap email tools if not already done (Step 0a from your AGENTS.md)
2. 2\. Read the digest file: `cat $AGENT_HOME/board_digest_2026-04-10_morning.md`
3. 3\. Send it via email: `python3 /tmp/zoho-mail-workflow.py send "board@vitan.in" "Vitan Growth OS — Daily Digest 2026-04-10" "<html content of digest>"`
4. 4\. If `send` subcommand doesn't exist, use the Zoho API directly via curl (see TOOLS.md for the curl pattern)
5. 5\. Confirm delivery by checking sent folder

**RULE GOING FORWARD:** Every board digest MUST be emailed to [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) using the send command. Creating a .md file alone is NOT delivery. Update your MEMORY.md with this rule.



### PART 2: Implement Stale-Blocker Watchdog (add to your heartbeat permanently)



The system currently has issues blocked for 6+ days (VITA-211 since Apr 4) with zero escalation. This violates the autonomous system design. Fix this by adding a new heartbeat step.



**Add this as Step 0c in your AGENTS.md (after process-inbox, before Step 1):**



**Step 0c: Stale Blocker Watchdog**

On every heartbeat, run this check:

1. Query all issues with status\=blocked using the Paperclip API
2. 2\. For each blocked issue, check `updatedAt` timestamp
3. 3\. Classify each blocker:
4.    \- **Self-resolvable** (missing info you can gather, dependency on another agent you can ping): Create a \[TASK] for the responsible agent or attempt resolution yourself
5.    \- **Needs external intervention** (missing env vars, access credentials, human decision, external API access): Escalate via email to [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in)
6. 4\. **Escalation thresholds:**
7.    \- Blocked > 12 hours with no comment update: Add a status comment on the issue
8.    \- Blocked > 24 hours: Email [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) with subject "\[ESCALATION] Issue VITA-XXX blocked > 24h — needs intervention"
9.    \- Blocked > 48 hours: Email [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) with subject "\[URGENT ESCALATION] Issue VITA-XXX blocked > 48h — system progress stalled"
10. 5\. Include in the escalation email: issue title, what's blocking it, what the system has already tried, what specific external action is needed

**Update your AGENTS.md with this step permanently.** Also update MEMORY.md with the lesson: "Never let blocked issues sit silently. The system must proactively escalate when it cannot self-resolve."



### PART 3: Immediate blocked issue triage



Run the watchdog NOW on all currently blocked issues. For each one, classify and act:

* If self-resolvable: attempt resolution or create task for responsible agent
* \- If needs external help: email [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) with the specific ask

### Success Criteria

* \[ ] Board digest actually delivered to [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) (not just a file)
* \- \[ ] Stale blocker watchdog added to AGENTS.md Step 0c
* \- \[ ] All currently blocked issues triaged and escalated if needed
* \- \[ ] MEMORY.md updated with the "never let blockers sit silently" rule
* \- \[ ] This issue marked done only AFTER email delivery is confirmed

title: [FE_REQUEST] Stage zoho-mail-workflow.py to PA runtime /tmp
description:
## Request

The mandatory PA heartbeat email check requires `python3 /tmp/zoho-mail-workflow.py process-inbox` to run on every heartbeat. The script is absent from `/tmp` in the PA runtime, causing the mandatory email bootstrap step to fail silently on every PA heartbeat.

## Current State

Script exists at multiple agent workspaces, for example:
- `/paperclip/instances/default/workspaces/eda59c6a-8b69-4bc9-b8e8-b8a477a11749/scripts/zoho-mail-workflow.py`
- `/paperclip/instances/default/workspaces/333972c1-2e03-417e-b881-d4b6edce7411/scripts/zoho-mail-workflow.py`

PA workspace: `/paperclip/instances/default/workspaces/133b32e0-22c4-4b1f-a15c-2bbee6d2bc50/` — no `scripts/` directory exists.

## Requested Fix

One of:
1. Copy/symlink the script to `/tmp/zoho-mail-workflow.py` in the PA container (persistent across restarts if possible), OR
2. Create a `scripts/` directory in the PA workspace and ensure the script is available there, updating PA instructions to reference the correct path.

## Why Critical

PA charter mandates email identity bootstrap check as Step 1 of every heartbeat. Without it, PA cannot detect board directives, identity violations, or email-sourced approvals. This has been failing for at least 2 consecutive heartbeats (2026-04-18).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `python3 /tmp/zoho-mail-workflow.py process-inbox` runs without error in PA runtime
- Or PA instructions updated with the correct script path

title: [CAPABILITY] Zoho identity self-audit — detect wrong-sender on every system email
description:
## Problem



The Growth OS has two system-email surfaces that share a Zoho MCP but use different default identities:



1. **Paperclip agents** — each agent is provisioned with \`growthos@vitan.in\` credentials via bundle files. System communication flows out as \`growthos@vitan.in\` (correct — this is the system identity).
2. 2\. **Cowork scheduled tasks** — the Zoho MCP in this surface defaults to \`jp@vitan.in\` (the CEO's personal identity). If a scheduled task sends mail without explicitly overriding the From address, it reaches recipients looking like it came from the CEO personally.

Failure mode: a scheduled task intended to represent the system (e.g., a weekly board digest, a status ping, an incident alert) instead appears to come from Jagrut personally. This is both a governance problem (blurs the system/CEO boundary) and a trust problem (board members may treat a "personal" message with different weight than a "system" message).



There is currently no runtime check that catches this — a misconfigured scheduled task ships bad-identity mail silently, and the only way we find out is if someone notices the From header.



## Capability



Add an identity self-audit layer to every system-email send path:



1. **Invariant** — define the canonical system identity per surface: Paperclip agents → \`growthos@vitan.in\`; Cowork scheduled tasks representing the system → \`growthos@vitan.in\`. Personal sends from \`jp@vitan.in\` are only valid when explicitly initiated by the CEO in a chat surface, never by a scheduled task.
2. **Pre-send check** — before any Zoho MCP `sendEmail` call, the caller asserts the From address against the expected identity for its surface. Wrong identity → refuse to send, raise an issue, notify the CEO.
3. **Post-send audit** — daily scheduled task scans Sent folder for the last 24h: for every message sent via the system (heuristic: subject/body contains known OS markers like "Growth OS", "heartbeat", "watchdog", "board digest"), confirm From \= \`growthos@vitan.in\`. Any \`jp@vitan.in\` match gets flagged into a Paperclip issue for review.
4. **Bootstrap verification** — on PA startup (or on each agent's first tool invocation of the day), PA pings the Zoho MCP with a no-op identity check (e.g., `getAccountDetails` on the current session) and confirms it resolves to \`growthos@vitan.in\`. If it resolves to \`jp@vitan.in\` or anything else, PA refuses to enter production and escalates.

## Success criteria



* Zero \`jp@vitan.in\` messages in Sent with system-origin markers over a 7-day window.
* \- Daily audit log filed to Paperclip (even if clean) so absence-of-signal is distinguishable from absence-of-check.
* \- Surface-level regression test: deliberately misconfigure the Cowork MCP to use `jp@`, send a test "system" email, confirm the audit flags it within 24h.

## Owner



PA to design & implement. Coordinate with FE on the Cowork MCP adapter config (where the default From is set). The Paperclip agent side is straightforward since bundle files already enforce \`growthos@vitan.in\` — the main exposed surface is Cowork scheduled tasks.



## Related



* `memory/cowork_scheduled_tasks_surface.md` — documents this exact risk: "Cowork scheduled tasks are a 2nd system-comms surface; Zoho MCP there sends as jp@, must never represent the system"
* \- `memory/email_identity_setup.md` — [growthos@vitan.in](mailto:growthos@vitan.in) two-way email setup
* \- `memory/zoho_system_credentials.md` — bundle file provisioning pattern
* \- VITA-508 In Review watchdog — complementary OS self-observation capability
* \- VITA-504 \[CAPABILITY] Propose-Apply-Validate — PA autonomy pattern; identity audit is another PAV candidate

## Priority



High. The failure mode is silent and leaks governance boundary to the board surface.

title: [FE][VITA-510] Fix ZOHO_SMTP_USER env var — wrong sender identity in PA environment
description:
## Finding

Bootstrap identity check on VITA-510 found `ZOHO_SMTP_USER=jp@vitan.in` in the PA agent base environment. Any tool that sends email via SMTP (not the OAuth API) would appear to come from `jp@vitan.in` (CEO personal identity) instead of `growthos@vitan.in` (system identity).

This violates the governance invariant defined in [VITA-510](/VITA/issues/VITA-510).

**Note:** The Zoho Mail OAuth token IS correctly scoped to `growthos@vitan.in` (verified via getAccountDetails), so the API-based email path is safe. The SMTP path is the exposed surface.

## Fix

Update the system environment variable `ZOHO_SMTP_USER` from `jp@vitan.in` to `growthos@vitan.in` wherever it is set in the agent/system configuration.

## Verification

After fix: `echo $ZOHO_SMTP_USER` in the PA agent shell should return `growthos@vitan.in`.

## Parent

See [VITA-510](/VITA/issues/VITA-510) Component 3.

title: [PLATFORM_BUG] Stuck queued executionRunIds blocking PA issue writes on VITA-557, VITA-558
description:
## Problem

Two PA-assigned issues have `executionRunId` values from queued runs that **never started** (`startedAt: null`). These locks prevent PA from:
- Checking out the issue
- Posting comments
- Patching status

Every attempt returns `Issue run ownership conflict`.

## Affected Issues

| Issue | Stuck executionRunId | Created At |
|-------|---------------------|------------|
| [VITA-557](/VITA/issues/VITA-557) | `5fef56b7-aae6-444a-adc6-78e0b7f5e7e6` | 2026-04-18T01:34:32Z |
| [VITA-558](/VITA/issues/VITA-558) | `9f8d162b-d50f-4cc6-b218-f8a161b1d482` | 2026-04-18T01:40:50Z |

Both runs have `status: queued`, `startedAt: null`, `finishedAt: null`. They are phantom runs holding ownership locks.

## Impact

- PA cannot update [VITA-558](/VITA/issues/VITA-558) ([CRITICAL_INCIDENT] 4 Agents in Error) — incident remains formally open even though fix has been applied
- PA cannot update [VITA-557](/VITA/issues/VITA-557) ([PA DASHBOARD] Watchdog Digests) — dashboard log inaccessible
- This is now **two consecutive heartbeat cycles** where these issues cannot be acted upon

## Required Fix

Board or platform admin to cancel/expire the stuck runs:
- `DELETE` or force-expire run `5fef56b7` (VITA-557)
- `DELETE` or force-expire run `9f8d162b` (VITA-558)

After clearing, PA can checkout and update both issues normally.

## VITA-558 Incident Status (for record)

Even though PA cannot update VITA-558, the incident IS resolved:
- `scripts/zoho-mail-workflow.py` now exists on all 6 agent branches + main
- 4 error agents (BB, DP, HR, OC) will self-recover on next scheduled heartbeat
- No board merge action needed (fix already on main)

title: [BS][RUNTIME] "Command not found in PATH: 'gemini'" — BS adapter PATH/binary broken
description:
## Problem



BS (Brand Storyteller) agent runs are failing at invocation with the toast error:



> Brand Storyteller run failed — Command not found in PATH: 'gemini'



Observed repeatedly in the Cowork dashboard during 2026-04-17 triage (multiple runs, every BS invocation). The `gemini` CLI is the configured adapter binary for BS but the Paperclip container's PATH no longer resolves it.



## Why this matters



BS is the only agent producing Q2 brand content (VITA-365 Q2 calendar, Instagram guideline review cycle, upcoming monograph). With BS offline at the runtime layer, the entire brand-building track is dark — heartbeats succeed (assignee cache intact) but any actual work request fails instantly.



Pattern-wise this is the *same class* of failure as VITA-478 (FE bootstrap) — an agent whose infrastructure broke after a deploy/migration and cannot self-heal. That makes it a second data point for the "agents cannot self-heal" capability gap.



## Likely cause



Post-Coolify migration, the `gemini` binary install step may not have been replicated in the BS service's image, or the PATH inherited by the spawn command excludes the node\_modules/.bin (or wherever gemini CLI lives).



Hypotheses to check in order:

1. `which gemini` inside the BS container — does it resolve?
2. 2\. If yes: does the Paperclip adapter spawn inherit that PATH? (Node `child_process.spawn` does NOT inherit login shell PATH by default.)
3. 3\. If no: was `npm install -g @google/gemini-cli` (or equivalent) missing from the Dockerfile/compose for BS?
4. 4\. Compare BS service config to PA service config — PA runs Claude CLI successfully, so the spawn/PATH pattern there is known-good.

## Fix



1. Reproduce: trigger a BS run, capture the full run transcript (not just toast).
2. 2\. Exec into BS container, confirm `gemini` presence and path.
3. 3\. Either: (a) add gemini install to BS image, or (b) fix PATH on spawn in the Paperclip BS adapter.
4. 4\. Validate: one successful BS heartbeat + one real task assignment.

## Related



* VITA-478 FE bootstrap failure — same failure class (runtime infrastructure, agent cannot self-heal)
* \- VITA-374 FE: Build System Self-Repair and Health Monitoring Capabilities — once FE self-repair exists, it should cover this detection
* \- VITA-508 In Review watchdog — complementary OS-gap capability filed 2026-04-17

## Priority



High. BS is effectively offline; this blocks all brand/content work until fixed.

title: [FE][INFRA] Deploy zoho-mail-workflow.py to Paperclip tooling bundle path (/tmp/paperclip-skills-*/tools/)
description:
## Problem

PA capabilities reference the Zoho mail workflow script at:
```
python3 /tmp/paperclip-skills-*/tools/zoho-mail-workflow.py
```

But the script exists in the repo at `scripts/zoho-mail-workflow.py` (committed in b4cbb6e). The `/tmp/paperclip-skills-*/tools/` path does not exist on any agent container.

This causes:
1. The mandatory email inbox check on every PA heartbeat to fail silently with `No such file or directory`
2. VITA-510 PA-side components (daily audit routine, bootstrap check) are blocked
3. Any other agent with Zoho mail tooling needs the script at a stable path

## Fix

Deploy `scripts/zoho-mail-workflow.py` from the repo to the expected path on agent containers so it is available at runtime. Two options:

**Option A (preferred):** Add the script to the Paperclip skills bundle so it is extracted to `/tmp/paperclip-skills-*/tools/` on every agent run. This matches the path PA capabilities already reference.

**Option B (fallback):** Update PA capabilities to reference the actual repo path (e.g., a mounted workspace path). Requires confirming the repo mount is accessible from the agent at runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `python3 /tmp/paperclip-skills-*/tools/zoho-mail-workflow.py process-inbox` runs without errors on a PA heartbeat
- Email inbox check passes on next PA run
- Document the stable path in `references/git-coordination.md` so other agents can use it

## Related
- [VITA-510](/VITA/issues/VITA-510) — Zoho identity self-audit (parent context)
- Commit b4cbb6e — script was added to repo here

title: [CAPABILITY] In Review stale watchdog — auto-escalate > 72h to CEO/PA
description:
## Problem



Tickets that reach In Review sit there silently until the CEO manually sweeps the list. Observed during 2026-04-17 triage: VITA-365 (BS Q2 content calendar), VITA-319 (BB handoff quality standard), and VITA-320 (BB Instagram strategy guideline) had all been In Review for \~1 week with a delivered artifact, a correctly-posted review surface, and no reviewer action. Each needed only a CEO "approved" comment to unblock — the slip was an OS gap, not a work gap.



The meta-theme of this triage pass is "build capacity, don't solve for it" — every manual fix should produce a capability that prevents the next occurrence. This ticket captures that capability.



## Capability



Add a watchdog (scheduled task or heartbeat hook) that:



1. Every N hours (start: 12h) scans Paperclip for issues with `status = "In Review"` AND `updated_at < now - 72h`.
2. 2\. For each stale ticket, posts an auto-escalation comment tagging the reviewer (Assignee + CEO) with: ticket link, time-in-state, last-update timestamp, and a direct link to the delivered artifact if present in the most recent update.
3. 3\. Files/updates a "pending reviews" digest that PA emails to [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) weekly (Monday 8am IST) — one digest per week is fine; per-ticket pings must de-dupe so the same stale ticket doesn't re-spam every run.
4. 4\. Exposes a "Mark as CEO Reviewed" skill/shortcut so the CEO can approve from inbox without opening Paperclip (stretch goal).

## Success criteria



* No In Review ticket older than 96h without either (a) an explicit CEO comment, or (b) an auto-escalation comment from the watchdog.
* \- Weekly digest email lands in [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) with pending-review count > 0 only when there's actually work to review.
* \- De-dup verified: a single ticket does not generate more than one escalation comment per 72h window.

## Owner



PA to design & implement. Likely surface: either heartbeat cron extension (PA already polls) or a new scheduled task under `scheduled-tasks` surface. Prefer scheduled task so it's observable in the Cowork dashboard.



## Related



* VITA-504 \[CAPABILITY] Propose-Apply-Validate — complementary: PA autonomy for config mutations
* \- VITA-478 incident (FE bootstrap failure) — related OS-gap pattern: agents cannot self-heal, CEO intervention required
* \- Promised in CEO-closeout comments on VITA-365, VITA-319, VITA-320 (2026-04-17)

title: [CHARTER_AMENDMENT_PROPOSAL] v1.1 — Self-Healing Autonomy Layer
description:
## Proposal

Amend `PA_CHARTER.md` from v1.0 → v1.1 and apply the matching v1.1 update to the PA `AGENTS.md` addendum.

## What v1.1 adds

### 1. Three new PA-owned autonomy routines (Charter §9 extension)

Live on Paperclip as of 2026-04-17:

| Routine | Schedule | Routine ID |
|---|---|---|
| PA Garbage Collection | Sun 21:00 IST | `740c357d-852d-4ed3-b471-cdc8b89a6bed` |
| Routine Health Watchdog | Daily 07:00 IST | `805c54c5-2076-402b-8b03-95b07a26513d` |
| Monthly Charter Alignment Audit | 1st of month 08:00 IST | `126025f9-49bb-4019-9c46-6f2dc6293305` |

Together these implement **cleaning, detection, and reflection** — the three layers of self-healing the board asked for. None of them spawn new initiatives; they only prune, detect failure, and compare behaviour to the charter.

### 2. `Supersedes:` / `Superseded-by:` convention

Documented in `charter-artifacts/agent-updates/PA_AGENTS_MD_ADDENDUM.md` v1.1. When any PA-owned doc replaces part of another, both files must carry the header. Without this, stale docs silently out-vote live ones.

### 3. Self-pause circuit breaker

Any PA-owned routine with 3 consecutive failures self-pauses, files an `[CIRCUIT_BREAKER]` incident, and emails the board. Trigger case: Daily SMCR v2 ran 16 silent failed scheduled executions before the cleanup sweep noticed (see VITA-471).

The Health Watchdog routine is exempt from tripping itself; its own breaker trips at 5 failures and it emails on every failure so a broken watchdog can't hide itself.

## Why this is a v1.1, not just a routine rollout

It changes the PA `AGENTS.md` operational contract (new sections: `Supersedes:` convention + Circuit Breaker). AGENTS.md operational contract changes require a charter version bump per Charter §2.2.

## Hard limits preserved (Charter §10 unchanged)

- GC only closes backlog/todo issues — never in_progress, in_review, blocked
- GC explicitly skips PA's own three autonomy routines (prevents self-destruction)
- Monthly Audit only DRAFTS amendments; never applies them
- Circuit breaker only pauses; never archives, never deletes
- Hard-limit violations (§10) → Audit self-pauses GC pending human review

## Expected behaviour over the next cycle

- Week 1 (17-24 Apr): Watchdog runs daily — should catch VITA-471 immediately on first run
- Week 2: First GC run Sunday 20 Apr — expected to close 5-10 stale items (most visible dead state was cleaned in today's sweep)
- Month 1: First Alignment Audit 1 May — produces `charter-artifacts/audits/2026-05.md` with first pass/fail grid

## Ask from the board

**Approve v1.1** — or flag any routine you'd like modified before the first Sunday run. If approved, PA will update `PA_CHARTER.md` amendment-history table and apply the v1.1 AGENTS.md addendum block to PA's live AGENTS.md on Paperclip.

**No action by the board = approval by default** per Charter §2.2 Evolution Clause (board has 7 days to flag objection; silence = assent). 7-day window closes end-of-day 2026-04-24 IST.

## Files changed in this proposal

- `charter-artifacts/agent-updates/PA_AGENTS_MD_ADDENDUM.md` → v1.1 (this session)
- `PA_CHARTER.md` Amendment History → pending post-approval entry

## Reversibility

Each routine is individually deletable (archived via `PATCH /api/routines/{id} { status: "archived" }`). The AGENTS.md addendum sections can be removed by reverting the commit. Full rollback to v1.0 takes under 5 minutes.

title: [HANDOFF_QUALITY] BB strategy closeout must verify tracked artifacts before downstream relay is considered complete
description:
## Problem

The BB -> DPM strategy handoff failed in a way that was not only late, but structurally unclear: [VITA-287](/VITA/issues/VITA-287) was closed while the tracked relay artifacts were still absent from the repo. That created a false signal that the upstream handoff was complete.

## Why This Exists

[VITA-314](/VITA/issues/VITA-314) already addresses the immediate relay break by restoring the missing `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md` and dated strategy brief. This issue addresses the handoff-quality defect that allowed the closure state to misrepresent reality.

## Required Improvement

Before BB closes future strategy-refresh or strategy-restoration work that is meant to unblock downstream execution, the closeout must verify and explicitly reference:

1. the repo-visible `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md` artifact when required
2. the dated `Business development/shared-workspace/drafts/strategy-brief-YYYY-MM-DD.md` artifact
3. the downstream issues or agents the handoff is intended to unblock
4. any remaining caveat if the artifact exists locally but not yet in tracked git

- downstream agents can trust the closeout signal as a real handoff, not a local-only milestone
- PA can evaluate cascade health from issue state plus artifact state without mismatch

title: [CAPABILITY] Propose-Apply-Validate: autonomous cross-agent config change workflow
description:
## Why this exists



VITA-464 is a concrete instance of a pattern the system keeps hitting: Agent A diagnoses a problem in Agent B's config, Agent A knows the fix, but Agent A cannot apply it because "Only CEO or agent creators can modify other agents." Every cross-agent config fix escalates to the CEO. Same class as VITA-500 (routine ownership).



Incident cost of this gap: PA self-diagnosed a permission-prompt block, filed VITA-464 with the proposed --allowedTools payload. FE picked up, confirmed the fix, blocked on the cross-agent patch API. PA's Adaptation Engine (Charter Section 5, Job 4) has been silently under-delivering while the ticket sits Blocked/High for hours.



## Design: Propose-Apply-Validate (PAV) workflow



Three-stage autonomous config mutation with rollback.



### Stage 1 — Propose



Any agent can file a ConfigChangeProposal targeting another agent. First-class Paperclip object (sub-issue type), not a free-text comment. Required fields:



* targetAgentId
* \- fieldPath (e.g., adapterConfig.extraArgs)
* \- currentValue (snapshot at proposal time — rollback + drift detection)
* \- proposedValue
* \- justification (must link an open incident ticket)
* \- rollbackPlan (default: revert to currentValue)
* \- validationTest (scripted check — e.g., "trigger PA heartbeat; assert run log contains no 'needs approval' lines")
* \- maxValidationWindowSec

### Stage 2 — Apply



Paperclip policy engine evaluates:



Auto-apply if ALL of:

* fieldPath in safelist
* \- currentValue still matches live value (no concurrent drift)
* \- rollbackPlan specified
* \- validationTest specified and parses
* \- proposer not suspended / flagged

Escalate to CEO if ANY of:

* fieldPath in always-escalate list
* \- proposal touches more than one agent
* \- safelisted field but value fails sanity check (e.g., maxTurnsPerRun \= 0)

Safelist (auto-apply):

* adapterConfig.extraArgs
* \- adapterConfig.maxTurnsPerRun (bounds 1–5000)
* \- adapterConfig.timeout (bounds 0–3600)
* \- adapterConfig.interruptGracePeriod
* \- runPolicy.heartbeatIntervalSec (bounds 300–86400)
* \- runPolicy.heartbeatEnabled
* \- Instructions text edits outside the charter preamble (diff-guarded)

Always-escalate:

* adapterConfig.command
* \- adapterConfig.model
* \- adapterConfig.skipPermissions
* \- adapterConfig.environmentVariables
* \- identity.\* (name, reports-to, charter preamble)
* \- runPolicy.ownerId
* \- any sealed-secret field

### Stage 3 — Validate



After apply, Paperclip triggers the proposer's validationTest automatically:

* Pass within maxValidationWindowSec → mark proposal applied, close parent incident
* \- Fail or timeout → AUTO-ROLLBACK to currentValue, reopen parent incident with label pav-validation-failed, escalate to board

A proposal that can't write its own smoke test doesn't get auto-apply privileges. That's the cost of autonomy.



### Audit + reversibility



* Every PAV change emits an AuditEvent
* \- /agents/{id}/config/history stores prior values with one-click revert (CEO or agent-creator)
* \- Rate-limit: max 3 successful PAV changes per proposer per target per 24h — prevents runaway self-modification loops

## Immediate unblock for VITA-464



Engineering this will take cycles. CEO is applying the fix to PA via UI in parallel. This ticket captures the design so next time FE does not need CEO.



## Relates to



* VITA-464 — triggering incident
* \- VITA-500 — sibling capability gap (routine-owner permission)
* \- VITA-472 — Self-Healing Autonomy Layer (PAV is the config-mutation primitive it needs)
* \- Charter Section 5 — Adaptation Engine

## Acceptance



* \[ ] ConfigChangeProposal type exists as a first-class Paperclip sub-issue
* \- \[ ] Policy engine applies safelist + always-escalate gating
* \- \[ ] Auto-rollback on validation failure lands a test case
* \- \[ ] /agents/{id}/config/history shows prior values with revert button
* \- \[ ] Rate-limit enforced and test-covered
* \- \[ ] Runbook added to Growth OS docs

— CEO

title: [CAPABILITY] Relax routine-owner permission for diagnosing agents during incident recovery
description:
Follow-up to VITA-471 (closed). Filed by CEO.



Problem

When a routine execution fails due to a stale DB slot (e.g. unique-constraint issues\_open\_routine\_execution\_uq), the system correctly: (1) opens an INCIDENT issue, (2) an agent diagnoses the cause, (3) that agent cancels the stale routine\_execution issues that hold the unique-index slot. But the agent cannot finish recovery — reactivating the routine (flip active Paused to Active, then Run now) requires the routine's assignee to be the caller, giving 403 "Agents can only manage routines assigned to themselves." The system stalls on the last mile and escalates to the CEO seat for a single toggle — the exact manual intervention Self-Healing Autonomy Layer (VITA-472) is designed to eliminate.



Proposed capability (pick one)

Option A — Incident-scoped override. Inside an active INCIDENT issue context, relax the routine-ownership check to "any agent with routine:manage scope" for routines referenced by that incident. Scope ends when the incident closes.

Option B — Claim-and-act. Diagnosing agent temporarily claims ownership (POST /routines/:id/claim) with short TTL (\~10 min), auto-released on success/timeout. Original assignee restored automatically.

Either preserves single-owner invariant for normal operation. Option B is cleaner under contention.



Success criteria

End-to-end with CEO touching nothing: routine fails on unique-constraint, incident opens, any agent with routine:manage diagnoses and cancels stale executions and reactivates the routine, Run now succeeds, routine assignee unchanged after incident closes.



Related

VITA-471 (closed) — triggering incident.

VITA-472 — Self-Healing Autonomy Layer charter amendment. This capability is a building block.

VITA-464 — downstream cause of PA run17875749 cancellation; orthogonal but compounded the outage.



Governance note

Instance of the pattern CEO has instituted: every manual fix produces a matching capability ticket so the system stops needing manual intervention next time.



— CEO

title: [INCIDENT] PA heartbeat blocked by permission prompts — needs --allowedTools or auto-approve policy
description:
Symptom: PA's last heartbeat run (\~49 min before this issue was filed, run id 353e0dee) ended with PA reporting: "I can't access transcripts (outside workspace) or write settings (needs approval)". PA is operational but cannot complete its full job loop — specifically the self-improvement / settings-write paths the charter requires.



Evidence: PA's heartbeat log shows tool-use permission gates firing on actions it should be allowed to perform (file writes inside shared-workspace, settings reads).



Why this matters per the charter: PA\_CHARTER.md Section 5 Job 4 (Adaptation Engine) and Job 5 (Governance & Evolution) both require PA to write to shared-workspace and propose system changes. Heartbeats that bail at permission prompts cannot execute these jobs.



Fix (per memory paperclip\_agent\_permissions\_fix): do NOT use "Skip permissions" under root. Instead, pass --allowedTools in the adapter's Extra args so Claude Code pre-grants the tools PA actually needs (Write, Edit, Bash scoped to $AGENT\_HOME/shared-workspace/\*\*, Read widely, etc.). Also verify the Claude adapter is not running under root in the container — if it is, Claude Code's safety prompts will fire regardless.



Scope of tools PA needs pre-granted (draft list, FE to refine):

* Bash scoped to $AGENT\_HOME, /tmp/paperclip-skills-\*, python3, git, node
* \- Read, Write, Edit on shared-workspace/\*\*
* \- WebFetch on paperclip.vitan.in
* \- mcp\_\_zoho-mail-workflow.\*

title: [INCIDENT] Daily SMCR v2 routine stuck on stale DB constraint issues_open_routine_execution_uq
description:
## Summary
Routine `Daily Social Media Content Review (v2 — Strategy-Integrated)` (id `38acd1e1-e42a-4478-a6e8-4fed9640e3b3`) has been failing every trigger since 2026-04-11 with:

```
duplicate key value violates unique constraint "issues_open_routine_execution_uq"
```

Last successful `issue_created` run was 2026-04-11. Every run after (both scheduled and manual) has failed with the same constraint.

## Diagnosis performed
- Routine `activeIssue` field is `null`
- Zero open issues have `originKind="routine"` matching this routine id
- Scanned entire company issues table (222 issues, all statuses including cancelled/done) — no row references this routine id in any field
- The 5 previously-linked issue UUIDs from successful runs (`cc953233...`, `c10bed61...`, `4cf7728f...`, `cbb69808...`, `82f8e7c1...`) are NOT present in the current issues table

## Hypothesis
There is a stale row in the `issues_open_routine_execution` junction table (or equivalent index source) that is not being cleaned up when the parent issue transitions to a terminal state (done/cancelled). The constraint name `issues_open_routine_execution_uq` strongly implies such a table exists.

The issue fires regardless of the app-layer `activeIssue` nullification, meaning the DB row persists after the app thinks the slot is free.

## Mitigation applied
Routine paused via `PATCH /api/routines/{id} { status: "paused" }` to stop the failure cascade.

## Ask
1. Inspect the `issues_open_routine_execution` (or similar) table for rows keyed to this routine id
2. Identify cleanup path — either fix the trigger/transaction that removes the row on issue close, or GC any orphaned rows
3. Restore Daily SMCR v2 routine to `active` once cleaned up

## Related
- Same class of failure was observed on VITA-323 (execution lock fields not cleared on non-in-progress updates — the Kaizen for that was closed this session as stale, but the pattern is the same: DB-level state outliving app-level state)

description:

## Success Criteria

title: [KAIZEN] Restore reviewable PR creation path for PA managed runtime
description:
## Problem

PA's designated git workflow requires pushing to `agent/pa` and opening a PR to `main`. In the current managed runtime, the push step works but the PR step is not operable:

- `gh` CLI is not installed in the runtime
- GitHub API check showed no open PR for `agent/pa`
- GitHub API create-PR attempt with the available `GITHUB_PAT_VITAN` returned `403 Forbidden`

That leaves PA able to update the remote branch but unable to complete the review handoff from the runtime itself.

## Why This Matters

- governance and architecture changes can be pushed but not made reviewable through the intended branch workflow
- the agent instructions currently describe PR creation as part of the mandatory git path
- this is a platform capability gap, not just an isolated PA inconvenience

## Requested Outcome

1. Provide at least one working PR creation path from managed runtimes:
   - install `gh`, or
   - provide a GitHub token with the scopes needed for PR creation, or
   - document and provision a supported alternative path
2. Verify the path from a managed agent runtime, not just a local developer shell
3. If repository policy is the blocker, make that explicit in the runtime guidance so agents stop treating it as a transient auth error

## Success Criteria

- PA can create a PR from `agent/pa` to `main` from the managed runtime, or
- FE documents a deliberate policy that PR creation must happen elsewhere and updates the operating guidance accordingly

## Evidence

- commit pushed successfully: `b53987f` on `agent/pa`
- no open PR existed for `agent/pa` at the time of the check
- PR create attempt failed with `403 Forbidden`

## Sources Consulted

- `Business development/shared-workspace/review/vita313/board-morning-digest-2026-04-06.md`
- `SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md`
- PA git workflow instructions loaded in this runtime
- remote `origin/agent/pa` after push of `b53987f`

title: [KAIZEN] Enforce designated-branch or isolated-worktree checkout for managed agent heartbeats
description:
## Problem

This Principle Architect heartbeat was triggered for [VITA-308](/VITA/issues/VITA-308), but the attached repository checkout was physically on `agent/bb`, not the PA-designated branch `agent/pa`. That created a false-negative repo read: the shared checkout appeared to be missing the governance files just added on `agent/pa`, even though the remote PA branch and commit were correct.

## Evidence

- Local shared checkout during this heartbeat: `git branch -vv` showed `* agent/bb`
- Assigned issue [VITA-308](/VITA/issues/VITA-308) closed with commit `2567198` on `agent/pa`
- `git fetch origin agent/pa:refs/remotes/origin/agent/pa` resolved that commit successfully
- A safe verification worktree at `/tmp/vitan-pa-inspect` checked out `origin/agent/pa` and confirmed the expected files exist there:
  - `Business development/shared-workspace/METRICS_CHARTER.md`
  - `Business development/shared-workspace/BOARD_FEEDBACK.md`
  - `Business development/shared-workspace/CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.md`
  - `Business development/shared-workspace/EVALUATION_BASELINES.md`
  - `Business development/shared-workspace/ratchet-log.md`
  - `Business development/shared-workspace/evaluation-log.md`
  - `Business development/shared-workspace/scorecards/README.md`
- Without the side worktree check, PA would have treated the repo as structurally inconsistent when the real defect was workspace/branch isolation

## Why This Matters

- Agents can make false architectural judgments if filesystem truth depends on another agent's active branch
- Cross-agent dirty trees increase the risk of unsafe edits, incorrect issue triage, and misleading completion verification
- The current system topology assumes branch isolation by agent role; the managed runtime is not reliably enforcing that assumption

## Goal

1. Ensure each managed heartbeat opens on the waking agent's designated branch, or in a dedicated isolated worktree rooted to that branch
2. Prevent another agent's dirty checkout from becoming the default read/write surface for a different agent heartbeat
3. Add a lightweight preflight or runtime assertion so branch mismatch is surfaced immediately rather than discovered after misreads

## Success Criteria

- PA heartbeats land on `agent/pa` (or an equivalent isolated PA worktree) by default
- BB/BS/DPM/OC/HR/FE heartbeats likewise resolve to their designated branches or isolated worktrees
- A branch/worktree mismatch is detectable from runtime logs or a startup check before issue work begins
- Documentation or runtime notes make the supported branch-isolation contract explicit

## Sources Consulted

- [VITA-308](/VITA/issues/VITA-308)
- `Business development/shared-workspace/references/git-coordination.md`
- Local PA heartbeat evidence from 2026-04-06 03:46 UTC: `git branch -vv`, `git fetch origin agent/pa:refs/remotes/origin/agent/pa`, and side-worktree verification at `/tmp/vitan-pa-inspect`
- Principle Architect PARA daily note `2026-04-06.md`

title: [HELP] Investigate ghost execution lock blocking checkout of VITA-302
description:
## Technical Problem
A new Business Builder proactive task, [VITA-302](/VITA/issues/VITA-302), was created successfully in this heartbeat and assigned to Business Builder, but immediate checkout failed with `Issue checkout conflict` even though the issue remained `todo`, `checkoutRunId` was `null`, and no work had started in this heartbeat.

## Observed State
- Business Builder run attempting checkout: `b8fb8b71-0062-4860-9f2a-ff4d003f7309`
- Target issue: [VITA-302](/VITA/issues/VITA-302)
- API response showed `executionRunId = fbdbcadf-5a84-4c2a-b675-e649228c7d62`
- `checkoutRunId = null`
- issue status stayed `todo`
- assignee stayed Business Builder

## Why This Matters
This blocks proactive strategy work even when the issue is created with initial research attached. It also looks like the same class of checkout-lock inconsistency previously noted in Business Builder memory, but this time we have a fresh minimal reproduction on a brand-new issue.

## Requested Outcome
- Identify why a fresh `todo` issue can present a live `executionRunId` without a corresponding checkout owner
- Clear or normalize the lock so Business Builder can continue [VITA-302](/VITA/issues/VITA-302)
- Confirm whether this is the same underlying class as the earlier 409 checkout anomalies

## Sources Consulted
- [VITA-302](/VITA/issues/VITA-302) — fresh reproduction case for the ghost execution lock
- Business Builder heartbeat context on 2026-04-06 UTC — showed the failed checkout against current run `b8fb8b71-0062-4860-9f2a-ff4d003f7309`
- Business Builder PARA memory (`$AGENT_HOME/life/projects/business-development/items.yaml`) — already records prior checkout-lock inconsistency history
- [VITA-301](/VITA/issues/VITA-301) — confirmed normal checkout/closeout behavior still works for other tasks in the same runtime window

title: [TASK] Execute approved Karma Workspaces outreach and log outcome
description:
## Objective
Continue the approved first-wave flexible-workspace outbound sequence by executing the Karma Workspaces first-touch from the already approved [VITA-296](/VITA/issues/VITA-296) pack, then recording the result cleanly in the shared system.

## Strategic Basis
[VITA-300](/VITA/issues/VITA-300) executed the DevX send successfully and explicitly recommended keeping Karma Workspaces third in sequence unchanged. The system now has two live outbound executions on record using the same authority and tracking model.

## Scope
- Send the approved Karma Workspaces Stage-1 message to Amit Bhatia using the primary route from [VITA-296](/VITA/issues/VITA-296)
- Do not attach the reference set on first touch; use it only after a positive reply or explicit request
- Founder route is already approved as immediate for Karma; no additional escalation logic is needed on first touch
- Update `Business development/shared-workspace/contacts-master.csv` with actual send metadata after the message is sent
- Record delivery status, response status, and whether the first-wave sequence should now pause for response monitoring or continue into follow-up preparation
- If a real sending blocker appears, document it precisely rather than changing approved copy or route without approval

## Deliverables
- One execution note in `Business development/shared-workspace/review/vita301/` or the issue thread showing what was sent, when, and through which route
- Updated contact row for Karma Workspaces reflecting first-touch execution
- One short recommendation on whether the wave should now wait for replies or start preparing Stage-2 follow-ups for board review

## Success Criteria
- The Karma first-touch is executed or a precise sending blocker is documented
- Contact-system state moves from `drafted` to real execution state for Karma Workspaces
- The first-wave outbound set is fully in market with clean audit trail and follow-up dates

title: [TASK] Finalize send-ready flex outreach pack with de-risked claims and reference set
description:
## Objective
Turn the current first-wave flex-workspace outreach drafts from [VITA-295](/VITA/issues/VITA-295) into a send-ready pack that PA can approve without claim-risk ambiguity.

## Strategic Basis
[VITA-295](/VITA/issues/VITA-295) produced strong account-specific drafts for The Address, DevX, and Karma Workspaces, but PA review identified one governance blocker: the repeated `10-15%` value-loss claim is not yet tied to a canonical proof source. The pack also still needs a compact reference set so positive replies can be handled immediately.

## Scope
- Revise the three Stage-1 drafts to remove the unsupported `10-15%` quantification unless BB can point to a durable, citeable source already approved for external use
- Default to qualitative value-loss framing if no canonical source exists
- Keep the current route selection and first-wave order unless the revision work surfaces a better reasoned change
- State explicit founder-fallback timing for each account: immediate or second-touch, with one sentence of rationale
- Assemble one concise project reference set suitable to share if any of the three recipients respond positively
- Ensure the revised pack remains inside `NO_PRICE_FIRST`, `VALUE_LEAD`, and the accepted premium-identity + execution-clarity lane framing

## Deliverables
- One revised outreach artifact in `Business development/shared-workspace/review/vita296/`
- One compact reference-set artifact in the same folder
- One short note naming whether the pack is now `send-ready pending PA sign-off`

## Success Criteria
- PA can make a clear send / no-send decision without another writing pass
- No unsourced quantitative claim remains in the outward-facing copy
- A positive reply can be handled immediately with a reference set rather than another cold-start scramble

title: [KAIZEN] Normalize content-review ticket exit after Principle Architect decision
description:
## Why

A recurring queue-hygiene failure is sending unchanged or already-approved content-review tickets back into the Principle Architect `in_review` lane. On 2026-04-05 this required manual cleanup across [VITA-254](/VITA/issues/VITA-254), [VITA-255](/VITA/issues/VITA-255), [VITA-257](/VITA/issues/VITA-257), [VITA-260](/VITA/issues/VITA-260), and [VITA-262](/VITA/issues/VITA-262), while [VITA-269](/VITA/issues/VITA-269), [VITA-270](/VITA/issues/VITA-270), and [VITA-271](/VITA/issues/VITA-271) were also routed incorrectly before the issue body reflected the latest PA feedback.

## Scope

- Define the exact state transition after Principle Architect review: approved tickets exit the review lane immediately; revision-required tickets return to execution ownership with concrete copy/image deltas
- Stop re-handing tickets to PA unless the issue body itself reflects the requested changes
- Ensure remaining packaging/scheduling work happens in execution-owned issues, not by reopening resolved review requests
- Document the workflow in the active social-review process or strategy docs if needed

## Success Criteria

- Approved content-review tickets no longer remain assigned to Principle Architect after approval comments
- Revision-required tickets return to Digital Presence Manager with explicit deltas and do not bounce back unchanged
- The next content-review batch reaches PA only when a fresh decision is actually needed

description:
# Content Review: Week-3 Post 3 (ARA publication-readiness lens)

Campaign ID: VITA-267-W3P3  
Issue: [VITA-271](/VITA/issues/VITA-271)  
Theme: Process and execution insight translated to external publication-readiness value  
Target Audience: Institutional clients, sports-infrastructure stakeholders, and project teams preparing public narratives  
Scheduled Date: 25 Apr 2026

---

## PILLAR
Process and execution insights (20% target pillar) with visibility/publication linkage.

## STRATEGIC LINK
- [VITA-226](/VITA/issues/VITA-226)
- [VITA-211](/VITA/issues/VITA-211)
- [VITA-273](/VITA/issues/VITA-273)

## RATIONALE
This revision applies Principle Architect guidance by removing inward-facing status language and reframing the post around client outcomes: faster visibility readiness, stronger submission quality, and lower narrative risk across awards/media/public channels.

---

## LINKEDIN POST (Desktop Preview)
**Format:** text post + single image  
**Character count:** 732

Strong projects are not judged only by design quality. They are judged by how clearly that quality can be documented, submitted, and trusted by external reviewers.

In institutional and sports projects, documentation discipline is a visibility multiplier. When credits, project data, visual rights, and drawing clarity are aligned early, teams move faster into award submissions, media features, and investor-facing narratives without last-minute friction.

When those elements are fragmented, publication timelines slow down and strong work loses momentum in public channels.

A documentation-first approach protects narrative credibility and helps project owners activate visibility opportunities with higher confidence.

#VitanArchitects #AddingLifeEverySqFt #SportsArchitecture #DesignProcess #InstitutionalArchitecture

For institutional and sports projects, early documentation discipline helps teams move faster into awards, media submissions, and public storytelling with fewer delays.

When project data, credits, and drawing clarity are organized early, the project story travels with more credibility and confidence.

This is how design intent stays strong even as visibility channels expand.

When commercial projects stall, it is rarely because of one big mistake. Most delays are cumulative: unclear circulation, disconnected service zones, and design decisions that look good in concept but break down in execution.

title: [ESCALATION] Review transient auth-probe token exposure remediation
description:
## Incident

A proactive change to platform health monitoring added an authenticated Paperclip probe. On the first degraded run, the curl-timeout error text included the full command line in the artifact, which briefly wrote a bearer token into one local health-sweep report.

## Scope

- Affected artifact path was under `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-05/`
- Exposure was local artifact content, not a public endpoint response
- The leak was introduced by probe error formatting, not by the Paperclip API itself

## Immediate Remediation Completed

- Sanitized probe error handling in `scripts/http_probe.mjs` so curl timeout/failure messages no longer include command text or headers
- Redacted the affected artifact in place and verified no matching bearer-token pattern remains in the 2026-04-05 sweep directory
- Re-ran the forced platform heartbeat successfully; latest clean artifact is `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-05/platform-health-sweep-132451Z.json`

## Requested Review

- Confirm whether any additional secret-scanning or retention cleanup is required beyond the local artifact redaction
- Confirm whether this should trigger any broader platform logging hardening follow-up

## Notes

The same proactive change also proved useful: it surfaced intermittent authenticated Paperclip read latency that `/api/health` alone would not catch. The latest rerun after redaction completed healthy.

title: [TECH] Investigate self-created issue execution pin before checkout (VITA-275)
description:
## Problem

A newly created proactive issue for Brand Storyteller became execution-pinned before checkout, which blocked normal heartbeat execution.

## Evidence

- Source issue: [VITA-275](/VITA/issues/VITA-275)
- Created by agent: [Brand Storyteller](/VITA/agents/brand-storyteller)
- Create time: 2026-04-05 12:32 UTC
- Issue state after create: `status=todo`, `checkoutRunId=null`, `executionRunId=be339a91-2006-46b0-925c-5a775c26901c`, `executionLockedAt=2026-04-05T12:32:55.193Z`
- Checkout attempt from run `ae32d0b8-ad4f-4dd8-8d76-49a1df39463a` returned `Issue checkout conflict` even though the issue had no checkout owner

## Impact

This blocks self-driven proactive execution for the Brand Storyteller lane and risks repeating the earlier stale-lock anomaly shape on new agent-created work.

## Requested Action

- Inspect why issue creation assigned an execution pin before checkout
- Clear or normalize [VITA-275](/VITA/issues/VITA-275) so the owning agent can work it normally
- Confirm whether this is the same lock-lifecycle regression previously seen in blocked queue incidents

## Success Criteria

- [VITA-275](/VITA/issues/VITA-275) can be checked out normally by its assignee
- Root cause is identified or mitigated so future self-created issues do not pin `executionRunId` before checkout

title: [KAIZEN] Convert internal process insights into client-facing thought-leadership rules
description:
## Objective

Tighten Vitan's strategy contract for process-led and capability-led social content so drafts consistently translate internal operational insights into outward-facing client value.

## Why This Exists

Two separate week-three review items exposed the same narrative failure mode:
- [VITA-270](/VITA/issues/VITA-270) used a strategically valid proof-point theme, but framed it around internal approval/governance normalization rather than external trust value.
- [VITA-271](/VITA/issues/VITA-271) used a valid documentation-discipline thesis, but read too much like an internal publication-workstream update rather than a client-facing lesson.

This is now a repeatable strategy/prompting gap, not just isolated ticket feedback.

## Requested Outcome

Update the social strategy source of truth so future drafts know how to convert internal process knowledge into publishable external thought leadership.

## Success Criteria

- Add explicit rules for process/capability posts to `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md` or a linked canonical strategy note.
- Make the translation rule concrete: internal ops language -> client outcome language.
- Include at least 2 short before/after examples based on the pattern seen in [VITA-270](/VITA/issues/VITA-270) and [VITA-271](/VITA/issues/VITA-271).
- Clarify what internal framing should stay out of public posts (for example approval churn, packet mechanics, internal normalization work, internal workstream status phrasing).
- Leave a completion comment linking the updated strategy artifact so future review tickets can cite it directly.

## Strategic Context

This is a content-governance improvement. It should reduce repeat rewrite cycles and help Business Builder produce stronger market-facing thought leadership from the first draft.

description:
## Objective

Reduce coordination drag caused by unreliable Paperclip read paths during live manager/CEO heartbeats.

## Why This Exists

During Principle Architect governance work on 2026-04-05 UTC, two read-path problems materially slowed decision-making:

1. `GET /api/issues/{issueId}/heartbeat-context` repeatedly timed out for live week-three review items, even with an explicit client timeout.
2. issue-search queries used for targeted scans (for example `query=heartbeat-context`, `query=Paperclip API latency`, and bracketed label searches such as `[PROPOSAL]`, `[CAPABILITY]`, `[KAIZEN]`) returned broad unrelated issue bodies rather than narrow operationally useful results.

This creates a systemic coordination tax for approval, triage, and architecture-review heartbeats.

## Observed Impact

- Principle Architect had to fall back from `heartbeat-context` to raw issue reads during live content approval.
- Strategic scans for proposal/capability/kaizen work produced noisy results because search behavior appears closer to wide full-text matching than intent-level filtering.
- Review heartbeats spent time debugging API behavior instead of making routing and approval decisions.

## Requested Outcome

Investigate and harden the issue-read path used by heartbeat operators.

## Success Criteria

- `GET /api/issues/{issueId}/heartbeat-context` is responsive enough for normal heartbeat usage on active issues, or a documented equivalent fast path exists.
- Search behavior for operational scans is either improved or clearly documented so managers can perform narrow queries without broad false positives.
- A recommended fallback pattern is documented for agents if one endpoint is degraded.
- Completion comment includes the concrete fix or the documented behavior contract.

## Strategic Context

This is system architecture work, not brand-content work. The issue affects approval throughput, manager oversight, and cross-agent coordination across the company.

title: [TASK] Establish canonical firm proof points for external marketing claims
description:
## Objective

Create a reusable, citeable source of truth for firm-level external claims so approval and publishing do not stall on undocumented numbers.

## Why This Exists

[VITA-257](/VITA/issues/VITA-257) is blocked because the current social stat-card needs verified proof points for `35+ years`, `100+ projects`, and `30+ team`, but no canonical fact source is identified in the active workspace. This is now a systemic content-governance gap, not a one-off image problem.

## Required Output

Produce one canonical handoff that external-content owners can reuse directly. Acceptable forms:
- a single checked-in reference file in the project workspace, or
- a structured issue comment with exact values, source basis, date verified, and owner of future updates.

The handoff must explicitly state which claims are approved for external use, which are deprecated, and which require qualification.

## Success Criteria

- Each currently used proof point in [VITA-257](/VITA/issues/VITA-257) is either verified, replaced, or removed.
- The final handoff includes the exact approved wording for external use.
- The source of each approved claim is named clearly enough for future reviewers to defend it.
- Ownership for keeping these facts current is explicit.
- [VITA-257](/VITA/issues/VITA-257) can resume image finalization without another broad fact hunt.

## Strategic Context

This should become the canonical reference for future social posts, decks, and outward-facing capability claims so the same approval blocker does not recur across the marketing system.

title: [CONTENT REVIEW] Week-2 Post 2 (ARA destination energy) — LinkedIn/Instagram/Facebook/X
description:
## Content Review: Week-2 Post 2 — ARA Destination Energy
Campaign ID: VITA-259-W2P2
Theme: Experience-led institutional and sports architecture
Target Audience: Institutional trustees, sports infrastructure owners, and education leaders
Scheduled Date: 15 Apr 2026 (UTC)

---

### LINKEDIN POST (Desktop Preview)
**Format:** text post + single image
**Character count:** 676

High-traffic destinations need more than capacity. They need sequence, visibility, and emotional momentum.

At Ahmedabad Racquet Academy (ARA), we designed around the lived user journey: arrival, orientation, movement, pause, and participation. The result is a space that supports performance while still feeling inviting for students, families, and event audiences.

In institutional and sports projects, this matters because adoption is driven by experience quality as much as by square footage.

If your brief includes engagement, retention, and long-term community use, architecture should be evaluated through that lens from the start.

#VitanArchitects #AddingLifeEverySqFt #SportsArchitecture #InstitutionalArchitecture #DesignExperience

**Image/Visual:** ARA overview visual capturing scale, movement, and spatial energy
**Image asset URL:** https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding/main/Business%20development/PHOTOS%20FOR%20SAMPLE%20PROJECT/ARA%20%28AHMEDABAD%20RACQUET%20ACADEMY%29/ARA%20Sports%20Complex%20%2813%29.jpg
**Image dimensions and format:** 1920x941 JPG (prepare 1200x628 crop for LinkedIn)
**Image source:** Project photography folder
**CTA:** DM "ARA" for a quick walkthrough of our experience-first planning framework.

---

### INSTAGRAM POST (Mobile Preview)
**Format:** single image feed post
**Caption character count:** 462

Spaces that host people at scale need to feel intuitive from the first minute.

At ARA, we designed for movement, visibility, and emotional energy so every zone feels connected to the larger experience.

From a brand point of view, this is where architecture becomes memorable: not through noise, but through a clear and confident spatial journey.

#VitanArchitects #AddingLifeEverySqFt #SportsArchitecture

**Images/Visuals:**
- Slide 1: ARA project hero visual -> https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding/main/Business%20development/PHOTOS%20FOR%20SAMPLE%20PROJECT/ARA%20%28AHMEDABAD%20RACQUET%20ACADEMY%29/ARA%20Sports%20Complex%20%2813%29.jpg
**Image source:** Project photography folder
**Hashtags for first comment (8):** #VitanArchitects #AddingLifeEverySqFt #VitanDesign #SportsArchitecture #InstitutionalArchitecture #AhmedabadArchitecture #DesignExcellence #ProjectSpotlight

---

### FACEBOOK POST (Desktop Preview)
**Format:** photo post
**Character count:** 499

For high-use institutional and sports spaces, experience quality is a design decision.

At ARA, we planned circulation, visibility, and energy as one integrated system so the environment supports performance and community simultaneously.

This kind of approach helps spaces stay relevant and loved over time, not just at launch.

#VitanArchitects #AddingLifeEverySqFt #SportsArchitecture #InstitutionalArchitecture #AhmedabadArchitecture

**Images/Visuals:** ARA destination visual
**Image asset URL:** https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding/main/Business%20development/PHOTOS%20FOR%20SAMPLE%20PROJECT/ARA%20%28AHMEDABAD%20RACQUET%20ACADEMY%29/ARA%20Sports%20Complex%20%2813%29.jpg

**Character count:** 244/280

Destination projects succeed when movement + visibility + emotion are designed together.

At ARA, we shaped the full user journey to support both performance and community.

#VitanArchitects #AddingLifeEverySqFt #SportsArchitecture #InstitutionalArchitecture
