title: [TASK] Catch up board review email for newly queued social approval issues
description:
## Strategic Context

The board review-send routine [VITA-253](/VITA/issues/VITA-253) correctly sent the first packet for [VITA-252](/VITA/issues/VITA-252) and [VITA-254](/VITA/issues/VITA-254). Since that send, Digital Presence Manager has advanced additional social approval work into the live queue:

- [VITA-255](/VITA/issues/VITA-255)
- [VITA-256](/VITA/issues/VITA-256)
- [VITA-257](/VITA/issues/VITA-257)
- [VITA-258](/VITA/issues/VITA-258)
- [VITA-260](/VITA/issues/VITA-260)
- [VITA-261](/VITA/issues/VITA-261)
- [VITA-262](/VITA/issues/VITA-262)

These items are now in `in_review` or `blocked`, but the board has not yet received a consolidated review packet for them. That creates approval-lane drift and risks publish-window slippage.

## Objective

Send an immediate catch-up content review email for the newly queued social approval issues, including explicit handling for any image-missing or conflict-marked items.

## Success Criteria

- Build and send one consolidated board-review packet covering all live review-ready content items created after [VITA-253](/VITA/issues/VITA-253).
- Include `⚠️ IMAGE NEEDED` or conflict flags where assets are missing or unresolved, especially [VITA-257](/VITA/issues/VITA-257).
- Comment on each included issue with the send timestamp and board-review status.
- Close this issue only after the send completes or move it to `blocked` with the exact failure point.

title: [TASK] Convert Week-1 social packet into board-review tickets before April 6 publish window
description:
## Strategic Context

The week-one social packet already exists in `Business development/reports/2026-04-03-vita91-week1-social-content-packet.md`, and its first publish window starts on April 6, 2026 (UTC). `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md` still requires Principle Architect approval before any external publishing, but the review lane is currently empty.

Evidence from today's live operating pass:

- [VITA-248](/VITA/issues/VITA-248) and [VITA-250](/VITA/issues/VITA-250) both closed as empty content-review sweeps.
- No active issue titles currently contain `[CONTENT REVIEW]`, so the board has nothing to approve even though publish windows begin tomorrow.
- The packet already contains six draft posts with platform priority, captions, hashtags, CTAs, and template/media mappings.

## Objective

Turn the existing week-one packet into actual board-review-ready execution items so approval can happen before the first scheduled publish window.

## Requested Outcome

- Create the missing `[CONTENT REVIEW]` issue or issues for the week-one packet.
- For each post, include final platform, caption, hashtags, CTA, selected media path, and board-review-ready raw public image URL when required by the platform flow.
- Ensure each review issue matches the current two-level approval model (content + image/visual).
- Sequence the review queue so the earliest publish windows are ready for board review first.

## Success Criteria

- At least the April 6 and April 7 posts are represented in live `[CONTENT REVIEW]` issues by the end of this task.
- Any missing media URLs or unresolved asset gaps are explicitly flagged in-thread instead of silently omitted.
- The resulting review issues are sufficient for the existing content-review routine to pick up and email to the board without manual reconstruction.
- If the packet itself needs strategic adjustment before review, leave a concise escalation note naming the exact conflict.

## References

- Strategy file: `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md`
- Source packet: `Business development/reports/2026-04-03-vita91-week1-social-content-packet.md`
- Empty-review proof: [VITA-248](/VITA/issues/VITA-248), [VITA-250](/VITA/issues/VITA-250)

title: [ESCALATION] Rotate GITHUB_PAT_VITAN for authenticated pushes to JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding
description:
## Escalation

Authenticated GitHub operations are currently blocked for `JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding`.

- Evidence from 2026-04-04 14:40 UTC: `node "$AGENT_HOME/scripts/github_repo_health.mjs" --repo JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding --json` returned `auth.status=401`, `health=degraded`, `mode=read_public`.
- Direct probe: `GET /user` with `Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_PAT_VITAN` returned `401 Bad credentials`.
- Repo-local git auth path for the Business Builder clone is now configured to read `GITHUB_PAT_VITAN` via `credential.helper`, so once a valid PAT is loaded into runtime env the clone can retry immediately.

Required board action:

- Replace `GITHUB_PAT_VITAN` with a valid PAT for user `JagPat` that has repo access to `JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding`.
- Reload/redeploy Paperclip so new heartbeats inherit the replacement secret.
- Re-run `node "$AGENT_HOME/scripts/platform_health_heartbeat.mjs" --force` to confirm recovery.

title: Adopt contacts-master.xlsx as single source of truth for all outreach contacts
description:
## Context

A new master contact sheet has been created at `Business development/shared-workspace/contacts-master.xlsx` (and CSV equivalent at `contacts-master.csv`). This replaces the previous `targeting-audience-master.csv` as the permanent, structured contact database.

## What Changed

### New columns added:
- **Contact ID**: VIT-C-NNN format (sequential)
- **Contact Type**: existing_client | new_prospect | known_contact | referral | partner
- **Relationship Stage**: cold | warm_intro | engaged | proposal_sent | won | lost | dormant
- **Priority**: A (send first) | B (important) | C (nurture)
- **Response Status**: not_sent | sent | opened | replied | bounced | no_response
- **Outreach Channel**: email | linkedin | phone | referral | in_person
- **Assigned Agent**: BB | HR | PA | FE | board
- **Draft Message Status**: pending | drafted | approved | sent
- **VITA Issue**: linked issue for tracking

### Sheets:
1. **Contacts Master** — all contacts with full classification
2. **Dashboard** — auto-calculated counts by type, stage, priority, response status
3. **Outreach Log** — every outreach action logged with date and outcome
4. **Agent Instructions** — how to use the sheet

## Agent Requirements

1. When discovering new contacts through enrichment or research, add them to contacts-master with a new VIT-C-NNN ID
2. Always classify contacts using the Contact Type and Relationship Stage dropdowns
3. Set Priority based on decision-maker level and company fit
4. After drafting outreach content, update Draft Message Status to "drafted"
5. Log every outreach action in the Outreach Log sheet
6. Never modify existing contact data without board approval — only append new rows or update status fields
7. The CSV version is for agent read/write; the XLSX is the formatted source of truth

## Pre-populated Data

The sheet already contains the 5 Week-16 contacts (VIT-C-001 through VIT-C-005) migrated from the old targeting CSV.

## File Convention

Per VITA-187, all contact-related artifacts go into per-issue subfolders under shared-workspace/.

title: [FE_REQUEST] Add Zoho Sent folder scanning to agent bundle — required by email identity audit routine
description:
## Request

The daily email identity audit routine ([VITA-642](/VITA/issues/VITA-642)) requires scanning the Zoho **Sent** folder to detect system-origin emails sent from the wrong identity (`jp@vitan.in` instead of `growthos@vitan.in`).

Currently, the agent bundle only configures the **incoming inbox** (`ZOHO_MAIL_MESSAGES_URL`) but not the Sent folder. As a result, every daily audit run produces PARTIAL status, which trips the circuit breaker after 3 runs.

## Gap

The following env vars / capabilities are missing from the `growthos@vitan.in` agent bundle:
- `ZOHO_SENT_FOLDER_URL` (or equivalent API path for listing Sent messages)
- OR: update `zoho-mail-workflow.py` to accept a `list-sent` subcommand that scans sent messages from the last 24h

## Required Output

Given a time window (last 24h), the script should return a list of sent messages with:
- `from` address
- `subject`
- `timestamp`
- `body_snippet` (first 200 chars)

## Success Criteria

- `python3 /tmp/zoho-mail-workflow.py list-sent --hours 24` returns JSON array of sent messages
- Daily audit routine ([VITA-642](/VITA/issues/VITA-642)) produces CLEAN or VIOLATION status (not PARTIAL)
- Circuit breaker for VITA-643 audit log is not triggered

## Also Fix

[VITA-643](/VITA/issues/VITA-643) (rolling audit log) was created with `status: in_progress` by PA’s routine, which auto-assigned an `executionRunId` that blocks subsequent comments. Admin reset needed: set VITA-643 to `backlog` status so the routine can post audit entries.

## Priority

Medium — audit is PARTIAL but not completely broken; circuit breaker gives 3-run grace period before self-pause.

title: DIM2/DIM5: ArchDaily Publication Path — Identify Project for BOTY 2027 Eligibility
description:
## Objective

Identify one strong completed project and push it to ArchDaily editorial publication by late 2026, making it eligible for BOTY 2027 voting.

## Why This Matters

- **No entry fee** for ArchDaily publication
- **Gujarat precedent**: Raj Sabhagruh (Dharampur) won BOTY 2026 Public & Landscape category via publication
- **Timeline**: Publish by late 2026 → eligible for BOTY 2027 voting
- **Compounding visibility**: Publication drives SEO for years beyond the award

## Strategic Framework

✅ **BB Input Complete (2026-04-10):** 
- Dimension 2 (Publication Route) criteria established
- Project selection framework documented
- Editorial pitch strategy aligned with SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md
- Downstream publication cascade defined (ArchDaily → ArchitectureLive! → ArchiSHOTS)

See comment thread for full BB strategic input.

## Project Selection Process

### Step 1: Candidate Identification (PA/Design) — Due May 10
- PA surfaces 2-3 candidates meeting publication readiness criteria
- Criteria: Quality photography, clean drawings, strong visual narrative
- Thematic fit: Institutional/public work with sustainability or efficiency angle preferred

### Step 2: Project Selection (BB/Board Review) — Due May 15
- BB evaluates candidates against ArchDaily editorial themes
- Board approves final selection
- Gate: If ARA remains publication-blocked, proceed with alternative project

### Step 3: Asset Preparation (Design + Photography) — May-June
- Publication-quality photography (12+ images with captions)
- Plans, sections, diagrams in presentation order
- Project facts sheet (location, year, typology, team)

### Step 4: Editorial Story (BS) — July-August
- 700-1000 word editorial narrative
- Unique angle: cost efficiency? sustainability? cultural impact?
- ArchDaily market research integrated

### Step 5: Submission (OC) — August-September
- Strategic pitch email to ArchDaily editors
- Track publication timeline
- Target: Publication by September 30 for BOTY 2027 eligibility

## Timeline

- **May 10, 2026**: PA presents candidate projects
- **May 15, 2026**: BB/Board project selection
- **May-June 2026**: Asset preparation
- **July-August 2026**: Editorial story + pitch refinement
- **August-September 2026**: ArchDaily submission
- **Late 2026**: Publication target
- **January 2027**: BOTY 2027 voting opens

## Success Criteria

- ✓ Project selected and approved by PA + Board
- ✓ Assets compiled and publication-ready
- ✓ Pitch submitted to ArchDaily
- ✓ Publication achieves visibility
- ✓ Project eligible for BOTY 2027

## Status

- **BB Dimension 2 Input:** Complete ✓
- **PA Project Candidates:** Pending (due May 10)
- **Board Selection Decision:** Pending
- **Asset Preparation:** Pending
- **Publication:** Pending (target: Sept 2026)

## Links & References

- [SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md](/VITA/issues/VITA-358#document-system-architecture) — Dimension 2 publication route discipline
- [VITA-369](/VITA/issues/VITA-369) — WAF 2026 coordination (parallel initiative)
- Awards Calendar: shared-workspace/intelligence/awards-calendar.md

## Next Action

**PA:** Coordinate with Design team to identify and present 2-3 candidate projects by **May 10, 2026**.

title: [INFRASTRUCTURE] growthos@vitan.in SMTP relay rejected — validation incomplete
description:
## Problem

The FE Liveness Monitor routine (deployed today, routine `aece8207-0b6c-498c-85cd-041a002fc6ad`) is designed to escalate critical failures by email from `growthos@vitan.in` to `jagrutpatel@gmail.com`. When PA tested the escalation path today by manually paging the board about the FE outage, Zoho Mail rejected the send:

```
553 5.7.1 Sender is not allowed to relay emails
```

PA's primary-address fallback (`jp@vitan.in`) works, so today's manual escalation went through. But the **autonomous routine uses growthos** per [email_identity_setup.md](memory/email_identity_setup.md), and without relay permission fixed, the routine's first real outbound email would silently fail.

## Root cause (hypothesis)

`growthOS@vitan.in` is configured in Zoho account 3228151000000002002 with `mode: extfrom_admin`, and `validated: false, validationRequired: true, validationState: 0`. Zoho's SMTP relay requires an extra domain-alias or sender verification step that wasn't completed when the identity was set up.

## Fix (board action required — HR can help coordinate)

Zoho Mail Admin Console → go to growthOS identity → complete sender-address validation (Zoho will send a verification email, the board clicks the link). Once validated, SMTP relay from `growthos@vitan.in` will succeed.

Reference: `email_identity_setup.md` — "growthos@vitan.in two-way email operational via PA bootstrap pattern" — inbound works; outbound from this identity needs validation.

## Impact if not fixed

Any autonomous board-escalation routine (FE Liveness Monitor is the first, but there will be more) would produce a silent send-failure. The board would not be paged; the routine would believe it succeeded until someone checked the Zoho bounce log.

## Workaround until fixed

PA's routine instructions will include a fallback: if `growthos` send returns 553, retry with `jp@vitan.in` as fromAddress and prepend `[growthos relay bounced]` to the subject. But this is a band-aid, not a fix — log this ticket so it doesn't rot.

— PA

title: [FE_REQUEST] zoho-mail-workflow.py — Layer 1 board-bypass classifier extension
description:
## Summary



Extend `zoho-mail-workflow.py` with a **board-bypass classifier** that flags incoming email where a board member is the sender AND PA is not on the recipients, and emits a `[BOARD AWARENESS]` Paperclip issue on PA for each detected message.



This is **Layer 1** of the three-layer board-bypass awareness system introduced by PA Charter v1.0 §9. Layer 2 (daily cross-agent issue scan) and Layer 3 (Monday standups) are already live on Paperclip.



**Full spec:** `Growth OS/shared-workspace/charter-artifacts/code-patches/zoho-mail-workflow-board-bypass.md` (WorkDrive-synced from Vitan-BrandBuilding repo).

**Charter ref:** `PA_CHARTER.md` §9 Layer 1.

**Branch:** `fe/board-bypass-layer1` on `Vitan-BrandBuilding`.



\---



## Contract (inputs)



* `BOARD_SENDERS = {"jp@vitan.in", "kaivana@vitan.in", "chitrang@vitan.in"}`
* \- `BOARD_GROUP_ADDRESSES = {"board@vitan.in"}`
* \- `PA_EMAIL_ALIASES = {"growthos+pa@vitan.in", "pa@vitan.in"}`
* \- `AGENT_EMAIL_MAP`: \`growthos+{pa,hr,fe,bb,bs,dpm,oc}@vitan.in\` → agent name

## Classification rule



A message is **board-bypass** iff ALL of:

1. sender ∈ BOARD\_SENDERS (or BOARD\_GROUP\_ADDRESSES)
2. 2\. at least one agent inbound alias in (To ∪ Cc ∪ Bcc)
3. 3\. PA not in (To ∪ Cc ∪ Bcc)
4. 4\. Thread root also did not have PA on recipients (so replies to PA-aware threads don't re-fire)

## Action on detection



1. Create Paperclip issue — prefix `[BOARD AWARENESS]`, title `Board-bypass email → <agent> — <subject>`, assignee PA, labels `board-bypass, layer-1, auto`, body with sender/subject/targeted-agents/Zoho thread link + 500-char preview.
2. 2\. Append entry to `/app/shared-workspace/board-bypass-log.jsonl` for the daily rollup (read by the PA cross-agent-issue-scan routine at 08:30 IST).
3. 3\. **Do NOT** auto-reply, forward, or take any other action. PA decides on next heartbeat.

## Idempotency



Use `message_id` header as dedup key against `board-bypass-log.jsonl`.



\---



## Prerequisites (verify before coding)



1. **Agent inbound aliases on** [**`growthos@vitan.in`**](mailto:growthos@vitan.in) — must exist: \`growthos+pa/hr/fe/bb/bs/dpm/oc@vitan.in\`. If not present, extend Zoho Mail alias config first (coordinate with HR — HR owns the growthos identity per `zoho_system_credentials.md`).
2. 2\. **Paperclip API client** — reuse the existing `create_issue` path already used by routing; do not duplicate HTTP plumbing.
3. 3\. **Write access** to `/app/shared-workspace/board-bypass-log.jsonl` (existing volume; confirm during rollout).

\---



## Reference implementation



See the spec file for the full Python reference (`is_board_bypass`, `emit_paperclip_board_awareness_issue`, `log_bypass`, `already_logged`, hook into `process_incoming_message`). Spec is reference-level — FE to integrate against the current structure of `zoho-mail-workflow.py`.



\---



## Testing plan



**Unit — `is_board_bypass`:**

* Board sender, only HR in To, PA absent → `(True, ["HR"])`
* \- Board sender, HR + PA in To → `(False, [])`
* \- Board sender, HR in To, thread-root had PA on Cc → `(False, [])`
* \- Non-board sender, HR in To → `(False, [])`
* \- \`board@vitan.in\` sender, HR in To → `(True, ["HR"])`

**Integration:**

* Send test email from \`jp@vitan.in\` → \`growthos+hr@vitan.in\` (no PA). Expect `[BOARD AWARENESS]` issue on PA within one poll cycle + log entry.
* \- Reply in same thread with PA on Cc. Expect NO new issue.

**Idempotency:** re-run workflow against same inbox; no duplicate issues.



\---



## Rollback / kill-switch



Add `BOARD_BYPASS_DETECTION_ENABLED = os.getenv("BOARD_BYPASS_DETECTION_ENABLED", "1") == "1"` and wrap the bypass block. Flip env var to `0` in Coolify to disable without redeploy.



\---



## Observability



* Log line: `board-bypass detected: <sender> → <agents> (issue=<id>)`
* \- Coolify log alert: keyword `board-bypass detected` → surface in FE Monday standup §1.
* \- Weekly metrics (trailing): bypass count, distribution by agent, Layer-1 vs Layer-2 detection overlap.

\---



## Cross-references



* Layer 2 (Paperclip-native): PA routine `cross-agent-issue-scan` — daily 08:30 IST (already live).
* \- Layer 3 (Monday standups): HR 10:00, FE 10:15, BB 10:30 IST (already live). Each has a §2 "what the board asked me directly this week" section.
* \- If PA adapter needs broader API scope for Layer 2, a separate `[FE_REQUEST]` issue covers that — this issue is Layer 1 only.

## Failure mode



If this fires false positives and spams PA, flip the kill-switch env var; file a follow-up issue describing the false-positive pattern so the classifier rule can be refined.



\---



**Estimate:** 4–6h including tests. **Priority:** Medium (Layer 2 + 3 give us coverage today; Layer 1 hardens it).

title: [KAIZEN] PA — Implement merge-cycle routine for agent branches
description:
**Agent:** Principle Architect (PA)

**Trigger:** VITA-320 incident — BB agent committed artifact locally but git push failed silently. Artifact was stranded on Railway container because no merge-to-main workflow exists. Agent branches accumulate work that never reaches main or users.



**Current approach:** Each agent pushes to its own branch (agent/bb, agent/dp, agent/bs, agent/oc). There is no automated or routine process to merge these branches to main. Users cannot access agent artifacts unless they manually check each branch on GitHub.



**Proposed change:** PA owns a merge-cycle routine with two triggers:



1. **Task-completion trigger:** When any agent closes a VITA issue and confirms a successful push, PA validates the artifact (content review passed, no conflicts, issue ref present, Sources Consulted block exists) and creates a \[TASK] issue for FE to execute the merge to main.
2. **Daily scan (10:30 AM IST):** During Daily Content Review, PA checks all agent branches for unmerged commits ahead of main. For any branch with approved artifacts, PA initiates the merge flow.

**Merge procedure:**

* PA validates merge-readiness (content review, no conflicts, valid issue ref, sources block)
* \- PA creates \[TASK] issue assigned to FE with branch, commit hashes, artifact paths, and merge style (squash)
* \- FE executes merge to main using PAT credential helper
* \- FE confirms merge with commit hash on the VITA issue
* \- Agent branches are persistent (never deleted), rebased after merge

**Conflict resolution:**

* Trivial (whitespace): FE resolves directly
* \- Content overlap: FE creates \[COORDINATION\_GAP], PA arbitrates
* \- Structural: FE escalates to PA

**Reference document:** git-coordination.md has been created with the full protocol (committed to repo).



**Success metric:** 100% of approved agent artifacts reach main within 24 hours of content review approval. Zero stranded artifacts on agent branches.



**Owner:** Principle Architect

**Review cycle:** Bi-weekly by board

title: [ROUTING_FEEDBACK] OC → assigning human-only tasks (photo upload) to FE
description:
# Routing rule: UPLOAD-type tasks should not be auto-assigned to FE

**Context:** VITA-474 ("UPLOAD: High-res photos for Shaligram Luxuria and Augusta") was created by OC and assigned directly to FE. This triggered run `8f0af423` which failed because FE's adapter is also broken right now (separate incident VITA-478) — but it would have been misrouted regardless.

## Why this is a routing bug, not just an FE bug

Uploading high-res project photography **from the board's local device to the repository** is inherently a human task. No agent has filesystem access to Jagrut's camera/phone archive. Even a perfectly healthy FE would have had to immediately comment "I cannot access the source files" and block the ticket.

## Routing rule to adopt

When OC creates a child issue under an existing KAIZEN/initiative:

- If the task involves **physical upload, scanning, photography, or retrieval from a board member's local device** → leave `assigneeAgentId` null and set a note like "needs human upload" in the description. PA's daily digest will surface it to the board.
- If the task involves **platform/infrastructure/deployment/adapter** work → FE
- If the task involves **content writing/editing/publishing** → BS
- If the task involves **social/digital channel ops** → DPM
- If the task involves **awards/external visibility/partnerships** → OC itself

## What I'd like you to add to your local memory

A short rule, something like: "Before auto-assigning a child issue, check: does the work actually require a specific agent's capabilities, or does it require access to a physical/human-only asset? If the latter, leave unassigned."

## No punishment

This is pattern-correction, not blame — OC was doing what made sense with the information it had. The board explicitly set up the Growth OS to be self-evolving (Charter §2.2), so surfacing and fixing patterns like this is the point.

— PA

cc: HR (capability registry) — if OC memory needs a formal capability/skill update to encode this rule, please scope it.

title: [KAIZEN] Clear execution lock fields immediately on non-in-progress issue updates
description:
## Problem

During the VITA-303 closeout, the immediate PATCH response for [VITA-303](/VITA/issues/VITA-303) still showed `executionRunId=3eceece4-f788-4e97-bcad-a9420f79c8e1` and `executionLockedAt` populated even though the issue status had already moved to `done` and `checkoutRunId` was null. A later GET after the next heartbeat showed those fields cleared, which means the runtime currently relies on heartbeat reconciliation to finish cleanup rather than clearing execution lock fields synchronously in the issue update path.

## Initial Research

- `/app/server/src/services/issues.ts` clears `checkoutRunId` when status leaves `in_progress`, but does not explicitly clear `executionRunId` or `executionLockedAt` in the same update path.
- `/app/server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` contains reconciliation branches that clear `executionRunId` and `executionLockedAt` for issues whose execution run is no longer valid or whose issue state is no longer active.
- Persisted state for [VITA-303](/VITA/issues/VITA-303) eventually self-cleared, confirming no live degradation right now, but the clearing appears eventual rather than transactional.

## Why This Matters

- The documented ownership/run-lock contract says non-`in_progress` transitions should clear run-lock metadata.
- Eventual cleanup widens the window where downstream readers can observe stale execution metadata on terminal issues.
- That can create false diagnostics, noisy operator reasoning, and future regressions in health-sweep assumptions.

## Goal

1. Make issue status/assignee transitions clear `executionRunId` and `executionLockedAt` in the same transactional update path that clears `checkoutRunId`.
2. Add regression coverage proving `done`, `cancelled`, and assignee-change transitions do not rely on heartbeat reconciliation for lock cleanup.
3. Revalidate that heartbeat reconciliation still acts as a safety net rather than the primary cleanup path.

## Success Criteria

- Immediate GET after a non-`in_progress` transition shows `checkoutRunId`, `executionRunId`, and `executionLockedAt` all cleared where expected.
- Regression coverage exists for terminal transitions and handoffs.
- Shared ownership/run-lock docs stay aligned with live behavior.

## Sources Consulted

- [VITA-303](/VITA/issues/VITA-303)
- `GET /api/issues/20f0bec9-4ce0-42ad-a0d5-986171a54157` after closeout
- `/app/server/src/services/issues.ts`
- `/app/server/src/services/heartbeat.ts`
- `$AGENT_HOME/reports/2026-04-06-vita303-ghost-execution-lock-runtime-mismatch.md`

title: FE: Build System Self-Repair and Health Monitoring Capabilities
description:
## Context

On 10 April 2026, all 7 Growth OS agents were found crashing on every heartbeat since ~8-9 April due to a permissions conflict (--dangerously-skip-permissions blocked under root). The fix was applied manually, but the system had no ability to detect, report, or recover from this failure autonomously.

## Problem

The agent system lacks self-repair capability due to:
1. **No health monitoring** — no agent can detect that another agent is crashing
2. **Circular email dependency** — the email pipeline depends on FE bundle provisioning, which was itself broken
3. **No service restart authority** — no agent can trigger Paperclip service restarts
4. **No external watchdog** — no monitoring exists outside the agents themselves

## Requirements

### 1. Agent Health Monitoring
- PA queries Paperclip API for last-heartbeat timestamps of all agents
- Flag agents whose last heartbeat exceeds 2x configured interval
- Add health status to board digest email

### 2. Email Pipeline Decoupling
- Fallback email path independent of FE bundle provisioning
- Direct Zoho API calls from PA using pre-extracted scripts

### 3. Service Restart Authority
- Provision Railway API credentials to PA
- PA can trigger Paperclip service redeploy via Railway API
- Safety: max 1 restart per hour, cooldown, escalation if unresolved

### 4. Escalation Ladder
- Auto-email board@vitan.in after 2 failed self-repair attempts
- Independent of main email pipeline

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PA detects crashed agents via heartbeat-age checks
- [ ] Board digest includes agent health status
- [ ] Email works even when FE is down
- [ ] PA can trigger Railway restart
- [ ] Escalation email fires after failed self-repair

title: [KAIZEN] FE — Add runtime secret-contract preflight for platform workflows
description:
## Trigger

Two separate blockers on April 6 exposed the same systemic gap: runtime-dependent work can reach live validation before required credentials are modeled and provisioned as a platform contract.

## Evidence

- [VITA-332](/VITA/issues/VITA-332) is blocked because `GITHUB_WORKFLOW_PAT_VITAN` was not provisioned when workflow-file push validation was needed
- [VITA-335](/VITA/issues/VITA-335) is blocked because `ZOHO_CLIENT_ID`, `ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `ZOHO_REFRESH_TOKEN`, and mailbox identifiers were never provisioned into a canonical source
- Board-side credential follow-ups had to be opened reactively in [VITA-333](/VITA/issues/VITA-333) and [VITA-336](/VITA/issues/VITA-336) after implementation was already complete enough to test

## Proposed Change

Add a platform-level secret-contract preflight for runtime-dependent workflows that records:

1. required env keys
2. canonical source of truth
3. target runtime mount status
4. provisioning owner when absent

The preflight should make missing credentials visible before live validation and should emit a precise provisioning issue instead of leaving the gap implicit in code or docs.

## Initial Artifact

- `reports/2026-04-06-runtime-secret-contract-preflight-gap.md`

## Success Criteria

- FE can declare secret requirements for key workflows in a single machine-readable contract
- A preflight check can report missing runtime credentials before agents attempt live validation
- Future blockers of the same class are caught earlier than [VITA-332](/VITA/issues/VITA-332) and [VITA-335](/VITA/issues/VITA-335)

## Sources Consulted

- [VITA-332](/VITA/issues/VITA-332)
- [VITA-333](/VITA/issues/VITA-333)
- [VITA-335](/VITA/issues/VITA-335)
- [VITA-336](/VITA/issues/VITA-336)
- `reports/2026-04-06-vita332-workflow-scope-push-path.md`
- `reports/2026-04-06-vita335-zoho-mail-oauth-source-gap.md`
- `reports/2026-04-06-runtime-secret-contract-preflight-gap.md`

title: [KAIZEN] PA — Implement board email digest and review workflow via board@vitan.in
description:
## Trigger

Board members currently have no automated way to receive work updates or provide feedback without opening Paperclip directly. VITA-320 identified this as a root-cause gap.



## Current Approach

No board communication loop exists. Artifacts sit on agent branches with no notification to stakeholders.



## Proposed Change

PA implements two email routines via [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) (group ID: 60069004476):



1. **Review Request Email** — sent at 11:00 AM IST after Daily Content Review
2.    \- Subject: `[REVIEW] VITA-{id}: {artifact title}`
3.    \- Body: summary, WorkDrive link to file in `Growth OS > Board Review > vita-{id}/`
4.    \- Board replies with verdict: Approved / Revise / Rejected
5. **Daily Digest Email** — sent at 7:30 PM IST
6.    \- Subject: `[DIGEST] Vitan Growth OS — {date}`
7.    \- Body: pending reviews, approvals, agent activity summary, escalations
8. **Reply Parsing** — PA reads board replies via Zoho Mail API
9.    \- First line \= verdict (Approved/Revise/Reject)
10.    \- Conflict resolution: most conservative verdict wins
11.    \- Logs all decisions in BOARD\_FEEDBACK.md
12. **WorkDrive Comments** — PA checks Board Review folder for inline comments
13.    \- Comments treated as "Revise" unless explicitly "Approved"

## Infrastructure Ready

* [board@vitan.in](mailto:board@vitan.in) group: CREATED (jp, kaivana, chitrang @vitan.in)
* \- Zoho Mail API: AVAILABLE
* \- WorkDrive Board Review folder: EXISTS
* \- GitHub-WorkDrive sync: CONFIGURED

## Success Metric

Board members can review and approve/reject artifacts entirely via email + WorkDrive within 24 hours, without opening Paperclip.



## Reference

See `board-communication-protocol.md` for full specification including email templates, parsing rules, and schedule.



## Owner

Principle Architect



## Review Cycle

Weekly — track board response times and feedback loop closure rate.

description:

- PA decision artifact: `Business development/shared-workspace/review/vita321/2026-04-06-dimension2-publication-route-decision.md`

- ArchitectureLive! / ArchiSHOTS is the default India-focused follow-on lane
- Surfaces Reporter is a conditional materiality-led route, not a co-equal default
- outlet priority and project priority must be treated as separate decisions
- execution should wait on the Brand Storyteller comparison when the first project candidate is still ambiguous

## Success Criteria

- the next dated strategy brief or canonical strategy update reflects the approved route order
- downstream execution signals for BS, DPM, and OC do not contradict the PA ruling
- the update explicitly avoids prestige-first route inflation

## Why This Exists

The planning cascade degrades when a board-approved decision stays inside a single issue thread. This task moves the ruling into the canonical strategy surface before the next planning cycle.

title: [EMAIL] Check growthOS inbox using process-inbox script

description:
SKIP your normal heartbeat protocol entirely. Do NOT use any commands with dollar-sign variable expansion — they will fail with "Contains simple\_expansion".

title: DIM2: Plan ArchDaily publication pathway for BOTY 2027 eligibility
description:
ArchDaily is both a major publishing platform (DIM 2) AND a pathway to the ArchDaily Building of the Year award (DIM 5). A project must be published on ArchDaily during the eligibility period before it can win BOTY.

**2027 BOTY Timeline** (from awards-calendar.md):
- 2026 cycle closed Feb 19, 2026
- Next practical milestone: ArchDaily publication before late 2026 / early 2027
- BOTY 2027 voting: eligibility window opens once projects are published

**Confirmed Gujarat Precedent**:
- Raj Sabhagruh, Dharampur, Gujarat won BOTY 2026 Public & Landscape category
- Ahmedabad work also appeared in recent BOTY candidate coverage
- This proves ArchDaily + BOTY pathway works for Vitan's market

**Immediate Action**:
1. Identify 1-2 completed institutional/civic/public projects with:
   - Finished photography (high-quality, editorial-ready)
   - Clear drawings (plans, elevations, sections)
   - Strong design narrative
   - Completion date within 3 years

2. Prepare ArchDaily submission package:
   - Project title, location, completion date
   - 300-500 word English description (compelling design story, not just facts)
   - 8-12 high-res images with captions
   - PDF drawings
   - Team credits & photography credits

3. Submit to ArchDaily editorial review
   - Acceptance rate: ~60% (quality + newsworthiness matters)
   - Time to publication: 2-4 weeks after acceptance

**Timeline**: Start prep in May 2026, submit by August 2026, expect publication by October 2026. This positions project for BOTY 2027 voting.

**Success Outcome**: 1 project published on ArchDaily, eligible for BOTY 2027, gains 50,000+ views from ArchDaily platform.

**Coordination**: BS prepares narrative, FE curates images and drawings, OC manages submission process and tracks publication.

title: [KAIZEN] Configure global git credential helper for GITHUB_PAT_VITAN on all agent workspaces
description:
VITA-320 exposed a systemic issue: agents that commit to Git and push using bare `git push` fail with \`could not read Username for https://github.com\` because the HTTPS remote has no credential helper configured.



The repo already has `scripts/commit_shared_workspace_artifacts.sh` with a PAT-based credential helper, but agents (codex\_local/gpt-5.4) don't always use it — they do their own `git commit` + `git push`.



Fix options (pick one):

1. Configure a global git credential helper on the container that injects `$GITHUB_PAT_VITAN` for all pushes to `github.com`. Add to container init: `git config --global credential.helper '!f() { echo username=x-access-token; echo "password=$GITHUB_PAT_VITAN"; }; f'`
2. 2\. Add a mandatory "Git Push Auth (Required)" section to every agent's AGENTS.md instructing them to always use the credential helper or `scripts/commit_shared_workspace_artifacts.sh` when pushing.
3. 3\. Set the git remote URL to include the token: `https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_PAT_VITAN@github.com/JagPat/Vitan-BrandBuilding`

Option 1 is the most robust since it works regardless of what the LLM decides to do.

description:

title: [KAIZEN] Add reusable outbound send helper or runbook for approved BB outreach
description:
## Objective
Reduce execution fragility in Business Builder outbound work by replacing runtime-only SMTP tribal knowledge with a reusable approved helper or runbook in the repo.

## Problem
The first flexible-workspace outbound wave succeeded, but the send path still depended on reconstructing SMTP mechanics, approval state, and artifact expectations from prior issue notes. That is a repeatable system weakness, not just a one-off inconvenience.

## Evidence
- [VITA-298](/VITA/issues/VITA-298), [VITA-300](/VITA/issues/VITA-300), and [VITA-301](/VITA/issues/VITA-301) all executed successfully, but each depended on live runtime SMTP knowledge rather than a checked-in helper or runbook
- The [VITA-301](/VITA/issues/VITA-301) retrospective explicitly called this out as the main remaining fragility after the first-wave batch went live

## Scope
- Design the smallest reusable mechanism that makes approved BB outreach safer and easier to execute next time
- Option A: a lightweight helper script with preflight checks
- Option B: a checked-in runbook snippet or documented execution checklist if a script is not the right first move
- Cover at minimum:
  - approval-state check
  - sensitivity / authority check
  - artifact existence check
  - canonical route / sender expectations
  - logging expectations after send
- Make sure the solution fits the normalized authority rule from [VITA-299](/VITA/issues/VITA-299)

## Deliverables
- One reusable helper or documented runbook/checklist in the repo
- One short note explaining when BB should use it and what it does not automate
- Any minimal doc updates needed to point BB at the new standard path

## Success Criteria
- Future approved outreach does not require BB to reconstruct SMTP execution mechanics from scattered issue comments
- The approved-send workflow becomes more repeatable without weakening control gates
- The solution is simple enough to be used on the next outbound wave immediately

## Sources Consulted
- [VITA-301](/VITA/issues/VITA-301)
- [VITA-300](/VITA/issues/VITA-300)
- [VITA-298](/VITA/issues/VITA-298)
- [VITA-299](/VITA/issues/VITA-299)
- `Business development/shared-workspace/references/engagement-system.md`

title: [KAIZEN] Add durable pagination and summary shaping for heartbeat run history
description:
## Problem

The board UI was effectively able to DOS the Paperclip control plane by requesting unbounded heartbeat-run history. Live evidence from `/paperclip/instances/default/logs/server.log` showed repeated `/api/companies/{companyId}/heartbeat-runs` and `/api/companies/{companyId}/heartbeat-runs`-adjacent board traffic pushing multi-megabyte responses and 60s-97s response times, which then caused local `/api/health`, `/api/agents/me`, and FE heartbeat sweeps to time out.

## Immediate Containment Already Applied

- capped the default heartbeat run list response to `100` rows
- capped explicit heartbeat run list requests to `200` rows
- restarted the supervised Paperclip child so the containment took effect live
- validated recovery: default `GET /api/companies/{companyId}/heartbeat-runs` now returned `100` rows in about `170ms`, `GET /api/companies/{companyId}/live-runs?minCount=5` returned in about `35ms`, and forced FE heartbeat artifact `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-05/platform-health-sweep-142032Z.json` was healthy

## Next Step

Ship the durable version of this fix instead of relying only on a hard cap.

- add explicit pagination / cursor semantics for heartbeat-run history
- define a lightweight summary response shape for list views so board pages do not depend on heavyweight run payloads
- update UI call sites to request bounded windows intentionally
- add regression coverage for unbounded-history latency / payload blowup so this does not regress silently

## Why This Matters

This is platform-health work, not a cosmetic optimization. The old behavior made the control plane appear down even when the process was still alive, which is exactly the kind of systemic agent-infrastructure failure the Founding Engineer lane is meant to prevent.

title: [KAIZEN] Prevent heartbeat auto-recovery from closing manual security-review escalations
description:
## Problem

A manual security-review escalation created under the platform-health project/goal was auto-resolved by the next healthy heartbeat cycle because the recovery matcher treated it like a managed health-fingerprint incident.

## Reproduction

- [VITA-277](/VITA/issues/VITA-277) was created to request Principle Architect review after a local artifact briefly contained a bearer token during auth-probe timeout handling
- The remediation was already complete, but the issue was intentionally asking for review rather than representing a live degraded fingerprint
- The next healthy heartbeat artifact `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-05/platform-health-sweep-132705Z.json` auto-resolved [VITA-277](/VITA/issues/VITA-277) via the managed escalation recovery path

## Why It Matters

Manual review/security escalations should not be silently closed just because the latest platform health checks are green. Recovery logic should distinguish live probe incidents from operator-created review tickets.

## Requested Outcome

- Narrow managed escalation matching so only heartbeat-created fingerprint incidents are auto-resolved
- Preserve manually created review/security issues until an explicit human or agent closeout occurs
- Add regression coverage for manual `[ESCALATION]` tickets in the platform-health project/goal that should survive healthy runs

## Initial Research

Current auto-resolution uses title/project/goal/creator heuristics that are broad enough to catch non-fingerprint review tickets. The matcher likely needs a stronger marker based on heartbeat-generated description/body structure or persisted fingerprint metadata rather than title prefix alone.

title: [KAIZEN] Deduplicate recurring platform-health incidents against canonical open threads
description:
## Problem

The current platform-health sweep is creating fresh escalation issues for the same unresolved `github.auth` incident instead of consolidating evidence onto one canonical recovery thread. That fragments ownership and forces repeated CEO triage.

## Initial Research

- [VITA-216](/VITA/issues/VITA-216) is already the active engineering blocker for Git push authentication failure.
- [VITA-218](/VITA/issues/VITA-218) was created as a broader platform-health escalation covering `github.auth` plus execution-lock anomalies and was routed to FE.
- [VITA-222](/VITA/issues/VITA-222) then appeared as another fresh `github.auth` escalation before the underlying incident was resolved.
- The repeated creation pattern indicates the health automation lacks canonical-thread linking or dedup behavior for still-open incidents.

## Requested Outcome

Design and implement a deduplication rule for platform-health escalations so repeated detections of the same unresolved failure update or reference the active canonical issue rather than opening another disconnected incident.

## Success Criteria

- Define what makes two health incidents equivalent enough to deduplicate.
- Update the sweep or incident-routing logic so repeated `github.auth` failures reuse the canonical thread while it is still open.
- Preserve new evidence artifacts and timestamps without losing incident history.
- Document the operator-visible behavior so future escalations remain understandable.
- Validate the new behavior against the current auth-outage case and link proof in the completion comment.

title: [ARCHITECTURE] Restore canonical system blueprint and planning-cascade scaffolding
description:
## Objective
Restore the PA-owned system architecture artifact and the minimum shared scaffolding required for the strategy -> weekly plan -> daily review cascade to operate without silent degradation.

- In the PA workspace clone on `agent/pa`, `SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md` is missing from the repo root.
- The repo also lacks `Business development/shared-workspace/drafts/`, so there is no canonical tracked location for `strategy-brief-*.md` and `weekly-plan-*.md` outputs.
- `Business development/shared-workspace/references/growth-os.md` exists and describes the older 4-workstream model, but it does not serve as the explicit 7-agent architecture blueprint requested in current governance.
- Current open work confirms the cascade is live but structurally fragile: [VITA-285](/VITA/issues/VITA-285) is active for DPM, while publication blockers remain open in [VITA-211](/VITA/issues/VITA-211) and [VITA-226](/VITA/issues/VITA-226).

## Scope
- Add `SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md` as the canonical PA-owned blueprint
- Add tracked drafts scaffolding for strategy briefs and weekly plans
- Align the architecture doc to the current 7-agent topology and planning cadence
- Record explicit artifact ownership so missing outputs become visible during review

## Success Criteria
- `SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md` exists in git and reflects the current 7-agent model
- `Business development/shared-workspace/drafts/` exists in git with clear usage guidance
- Architecture doc explicitly names the Tuesday strategy refresh, Thursday weekly plan, daily content review, and board digest routines

title: [KAIZEN] Add secret-safe regression coverage for platform health artifacts
description:
## Why

Follow-on from [VITA-277](/VITA/issues/VITA-277). The immediate token exposure was contained by sanitizing `scripts/http_probe.mjs` and redacting the affected local report, but the current coverage is still too narrow: there is no focused regression test proving auth headers never reach persisted sweep artifacts, and probe/report persistence still relies on local string discipline instead of a shared redaction boundary.

## Scope

- Add regression coverage that fails if `Authorization` or bearer-token-shaped strings reach persisted platform-health artifact output through fetch/curl probe failures
- Introduce or document a shared artifact-redaction boundary for persisted probe/error text so future authenticated checks do not rely on per-callsite sanitization
- Re-run the relevant platform-health script test suite and document the proof in the issue thread

## Success Criteria

- A test proves the curl fallback / auth-probe error path cannot serialize headers or bearer tokens into artifact JSON
- Persisted artifact writing has an explicit secret-safety guardrail, not just the current curl-summary patch
- Issue thread includes test command output summary and links back to [VITA-277](/VITA/issues/VITA-277) as the triggering incident

title: [TASK] Clarify canonical social hashtag and approval-ticket contract in SOCIAL_STRATEGY
description:
## Strategic Context

Principle Architect review of the live social queue found an execution bottleneck inside Digital Presence Manager's lane, but part of the drift is actually strategy ownership, not only execution discipline.

Current evidence:

- [VITA-260](/VITA/issues/VITA-260), [VITA-261](/VITA/issues/VITA-261), and [VITA-262](/VITA/issues/VITA-262) required Principle Architect approval comments directing pre-publish cleanup on hashtag packaging.
- [VITA-264](/VITA/issues/VITA-264) now covers execution-layer normalization in Digital Presence Manager's workflow.
- `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md` currently specifies an Instagram rule of 6-10 tags, but recent tickets exceeded that cap.
- The recurring branded hashtag form is not fully normalized across the active social queue, while the company tagline and brand references use the spelled-out form "Adding life, every square foot".
- Approval tickets are expected to carry the `Feedback alignment` block, but the live review tickets did not include it.

## Objective

Update `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md` so the canonical social rule set is explicit, internally consistent, and easy for Digital Presence Manager to apply without re-interpretation.

## Success Criteria

- Decide and document one canonical recurring branded hashtag form for social use.
- Confirm or revise the Instagram hashtag-count rule so it matches the operating expectation you want enforced.
- Add or tighten the approval-ticket contract so the `Feedback alignment` block requirement is unambiguous at draft time.
- Leave a completion comment linking the exact strategy edits and explicitly naming any downstream effect on [VITA-264](/VITA/issues/VITA-264).

## Coordination Note

- This is a strategy-owner clarification task for Business Builder.
- Digital Presence Manager owns the execution cleanup in [VITA-264](/VITA/issues/VITA-264); do not duplicate that work.
- If you conclude the current wording is already correct, state that explicitly and explain where the execution misread occurred.

title: [KAIZEN] Add identifier and assignee-user filters to issue queue queries
description:
## Problem

Recent blocker-governance work showed that queue inspection still requires extra parsing because the issues API does not provide reliable direct filtering for issue identifier or `assigneeUserId`. HR called this out explicitly while completing [VITA-240](/VITA/issues/VITA-240), where human-owned blocker aging snapshots had to reconstruct dependency state from broader queue results instead of querying the relevant board-owned items directly.

## Initial Research

- [VITA-240](/VITA/issues/VITA-240) succeeded, but its retrospective noted that lack of `identifier` and `assigneeUserId` query support slows human-owned blocker extraction.
- The new blocker-governance model now depends on seeing both agent-owned and human-owned issue states clearly.
- [VITA-245](/VITA/issues/VITA-245) already hardened ownership-aware anomaly detection; this follow-on would harden the query surface used by PA/HR unblock sweeps.

## Requested Outcome

Add reliable issue-list filtering for:

- exact `identifier`
- `assigneeUserId`

## Success Criteria

- Queue/API consumers can request issues by exact identifier without broad list parsing.
- Queue/API consumers can filter directly by `assigneeUserId` for human-owned blocker reporting.
- Existing issue-list behavior remains backward compatible for current consumers.
- A completion comment includes one concrete example showing a cleaner blocker snapshot/query workflow after the change.

## Strategic Context

This makes human-owned blocker governance operationally cheaper and reduces the amount of ad hoc query logic required in future unblock sweeps.

title: [KAIZEN] Add upstream-event gating to blocked-lane reactivation planning
description:
## Problem

Recent blocked-lane follow-up work showed that reactivation tickets can fire too early when they are created before the upstream dependency produces any concrete event. The clearest example was [VITA-238](/VITA/issues/VITA-238), where Brand Storyteller confirmed the dependency chain was explicit but the board/project-team request in [VITA-233](/VITA/issues/VITA-233) had not actually changed yet.

## Requested Outcome

Update HR's blocked-queue review / checkpoint patterns so reactivation tasks are only proposed when an upstream dependency has a concrete trigger such as:

- comment
- attachment or artifact arrival
- status change
- explicit human response

## Success Criteria

- Future unblock or checkpoint proposals distinguish between 'dependency exists' and 'dependency changed'.
- Reactivation tasks are not opened purely on anticipation when the parent issue already carries a current blocked state.
- Weekly reflections or health snapshots mention event-gating when recommending follow-up execution.

## Strategic Context

This operationalizes the architecture rule added after the [VITA-238](/VITA/issues/VITA-238) retrospective so unblock planning creates fewer premature wake-ups and less queue noise.

title: [KAIZEN] Publish issue ownership contract in shared API docs
description:
## Problem

Ownership semantics for issue run-lock metadata are now explicit in FE notes after [VITA-245](/VITA/issues/VITA-245), but the shared docs still do not describe when `checkoutRunId` / `executionRunId` are expected for agent-owned vs user-owned issues. During VITA-245 closeout, an older remembered operator-doc path also proved stale, which increases the chance of detector logic drifting away from server semantics.

## Goal

Add a small ownership-contract section to the shared issue API/docs surface that explains valid agent-owned, user-owned, and handoff states for `todo`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, and `in_review`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Shared docs describe when run-lock metadata is expected and when it must be cleared.
- Docs explicitly call out that board-owned `in_progress` issues do not use checkout.
- Docs link to the current lock-triage guidance or replace stale references.
- The doc update references [VITA-245](/VITA/issues/VITA-245) as the originating reliability fix.

## Initial Research

- Server semantics already clear run-lock metadata on assignee and non-`in_progress` status transitions in `/app/server/src/services/issues.ts`.
- FE note `reports/2026-04-05-vita245-issue-lock-ownership-matrix.md` already defines the detector contract and can be distilled into shared docs.

title: [KAIZEN] Expand ownership-aware issue-lock detection coverage
description:
## Problem

Recent platform-health incidents ([VITA-218](/VITA/issues/VITA-218), [VITA-243](/VITA/issues/VITA-243), and [VITA-244](/VITA/issues/VITA-244)) showed that issue-lock anomaly reporting has focused on stale agent execution state, but the live system also carries valid human-owned issue states that should not be classified as lock defects.

The immediate false positive is fixed: board-owned [VITA-233](/VITA/issues/VITA-233) is legitimate `in_progress` work with `assigneeUserId` set and no `checkoutRunId`, and the detector now skips that shape. The remaining systemic gap is broader coverage: heartbeat artifacts and tests should make actor ownership explicit so future changes do not regress mixed human/agent workflows.

## Goal

1. Expand issue-lock regression coverage for agent-owned vs user-owned issue states.
2. Make heartbeat artifacts clearer about actor type when anomalies are present or intentionally ignored.
3. Define the expected ownership matrix for `todo`, `in_progress`, and handoff states so future fixes do not reintroduce false positives.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test coverage exists for valid user-owned issue states that lack checkout metadata.
- Health-artifact output or detector metadata clearly distinguishes agent-owned anomalies from human-owned valid states.
- The expected ownership semantics are documented in the relevant FE technical notes or runbook.
- A forced heartbeat revalidation demonstrates no regression after the coverage/documentation update.

## Why Now

[VITA-240](/VITA/issues/VITA-240) already added age/checkpoint tracking for human-owned blockers. This follow-up closes the adjacent detection gap so human-owned blockers are tracked as blockers, not misreported as lock corruption.

title: [GROWTH] Build a two-week approval-ready social content backlog from existing proof assets
description:
## Objective

Use the current social strategy and already-available repository assets to prepare a short approval-ready social backlog that can move while board-owned data and credential blockers remain unresolved elsewhere in the queue.

## Initial Research

- Digital Presence Manager is currently idle while the highest-friction live blockers are concentrated in board-side and platform recovery paths such as [VITA-233](/VITA/issues/VITA-233), [VITA-234](/VITA/issues/VITA-234), [VITA-216](/VITA/issues/VITA-216), and [VITA-222](/VITA/issues/VITA-222).
- `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md` already defines usable content pillars, platform formats, hashtag rules, and the image pipeline for repo-sourced assets.
- This means backlog preparation can progress without waiting for new permissions, PAT repair, or founder-send actions, as long as the task stops at draft and approval-ready packages.

## Requested Outcome

Prepare a two-week draft backlog for Vitan's social channels using existing proof-safe material only.

## Success Criteria

- Produce 6 approval-ready draft packages spanning at least 3 content pillars from `SOCIAL_STRATEGY.md`.
- Include platform recommendation, caption draft, CTA, hashtag set, and candidate asset path for each package.
- Flag any asset gaps or approval risks explicitly instead of improvising around them.
- Store the working pack under `Business development/shared-workspace/review/vita241/`.
- This task does not publish; it only prepares a clean review queue for Principle Architect approval.

## Strategic Context

This rebalances idle execution capacity into a growth lane that is independent of the current board-side blockers and should reduce approval latency once the queue opens up.

description:

## Why This Exists

The speaking-opportunities research is already complete and tracked in the Business Builder clone, but the issue is still blocked on authenticated GitHub push. Founding Engineer has already noted that after PAT recovery, the branch will still require a normal sync/rebase step before publish can succeed.

## Success Criteria

title: [KAIZEN] Track human-owned blocker age and escalation checkpoints in health snapshots
description:
## Problem

Current blocked-queue reviews distinguish blocker type and event-gating, but they do not yet make quiet human-owned dependencies visibly age. Live examples are [VITA-233](/VITA/issues/VITA-233) and [VITA-234](/VITA/issues/VITA-234), which are correctly narrowed board-owned tasks yet still easy to overlook because they sit as waiting `todo` items without an explicit aging/escalation view.

## Requested Outcome

Update HR health snapshots and unblock reviews so human-owned blockers are tracked with:

- last request timestamp
- next escalation checkpoint
- escalation owner
- age bucket
- whether a reminder/escalation already exists

## Success Criteria

- Weekly or ad hoc blocked-queue snapshots show human-owned blockers as a separate section or clearly flagged subset.
- Review output distinguishes "waiting on human action" from agent-owned blockers that are already in active remediation.
- Follow-up recommendations prefer escalating the existing dependency path instead of spawning premature downstream execution tasks.
- At least one live example references [VITA-233](/VITA/issues/VITA-233) or [VITA-234](/VITA/issues/VITA-234).

## Strategic Context

This operationalizes the human-owned blocker aging rule now added to `SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md` so the system can see when a blocker is truly waiting on people rather than silently stalling.

That specific control-plane fix path has now been completed by Founding Engineer in [VITA-229](/VITA/issues/VITA-229), but the awards-research issue is still sitting in a stale blocked state.

title: KAIZEN: Add HR workspace-preflight rule to AGENTS instructions

title: [KAIZEN] Adopt queued same-agent execution run residue during issue checkout
description:
## Problem

Recurring self-checkout conflicts still appear on fresh `todo` issues where `checkoutRunId` is null but `executionRunId` is already pinned to a queued prior run owned by the same agent. Recent examples include [VITA-162](/VITA/issues/VITA-162), [VITA-176](/VITA/issues/VITA-176), and [VITA-227](/VITA/issues/VITA-227).

## Evidence

- Current `issueService.checkout()` source only auto-adopts terminal or missing execution runs for expected-status issues.
- The current source does not yet treat queued same-agent prior runs as adoptable in that branch, which matches the live recurrence pattern.
- PARA memory in `projects/run-lock-remediation` already records this repeated shape across multiple heartbeats.

## Proposed Fix

- Extend the adoptable execution-run check so checkout can take over a queued prior run when:
  - the issue is already assigned to the same agent
  - the pinned execution run belongs to that same agent
  - the current checkout actor run is already `running`
- Add a regression test covering the `todo` + queued-same-agent residue path.

## Acceptance

- `issueService.checkout()` no longer 409s on the queued same-agent residue path for expected-status issues.
- Regression coverage exists for both stale terminal/missing runs and queued same-agent prior runs.
- Existing cross-agent protection remains unchanged.

title: [KAIZEN] Provision PARA recall tooling in managed agent runtimes
description:
## Problem

- The `para-memory-files` skill requires `qmd` for recall, but the current Founding Engineer managed runtime does not have `qmd` on PATH.
- This forces manual `find`/`sed` fallbacks during heartbeat work, which slows recall and makes PARA usage less reliable under time pressure.
- The gap is systemic platform tooling, not an issue-specific blocker.

## Evidence

- `command -v qmd` returned no path in the current managed heartbeat environment on 2026-04-04.
- PARA recall had to fall back to direct file reads across multiple heartbeats while working the GitHub auth incident chain.
- No existing open issue currently tracks `qmd` or managed PARA recall provisioning.

## Proposed Fix

- Decide whether managed agent runtimes should include `qmd` by default or expose an equivalent supported recall command.
- If provisioning `qmd` is the right path, document installation expectations and validate it works inside heartbeat environments.
- If `qmd` should not be installed globally, add a documented fallback utility/workflow so PARA memory remains first-class without ad hoc shell spelunking.

## Acceptance

- A supported PARA recall path exists in managed heartbeat environments.
- Founding Engineer memory workflow no longer depends on undocumented `find`/`sed` fallbacks for routine recall.
- Agent instructions or platform docs clearly describe the supported recall toolchain.

title: KAIZEN: onboarding artifact path preflight before privileged handoff
description: Add a standard preflight checklist for HR onboarding tasks to verify that referenced artifact files exist in the active workspace root (`$AGENT_HOME/shared-workspace/...`) before creating privileged execution tasks. This prevents spec-file-not-found caveats like the one surfaced in [VITA-220](/VITA/issues/VITA-220).

title: [KAIZEN] Correlate shared-secret degradations with dependent git issues
description:
## Problem

- [VITA-222](/VITA/issues/VITA-222) shows the current runtime `GITHUB_PAT_VITAN` is invalid at the platform level.
- [VITA-216](/VITA/issues/VITA-216) still carries the older repo-local helper symptom, even though the active blocker is now the shared secret itself.
- Repeated GitHub auth incidents are costing time because downstream git issues keep stale blocker narratives until FE manually re-triages them.

## Proposed Fix

- Extend platform health handling so a live `github.auth` degradation can identify open GitHub-dependent issues and link them to the active escalation.
- Add one-time correlation comments or a structured dependency map so related issues inherit the current root cause and runbook without duplicate spam.
- Preserve dedup behavior: if the same degradation persists, do not keep reposting the same dependency comment every heartbeat.

## Acceptance

- A fixture-backed or live-safe test covers the correlation path for a `github.auth` failure.
- When the platform detects a shared GitHub credential failure, dependent git-push issues get a link to the active escalation and renewal runbook.
- No duplicate dependency comments are emitted while the same degradation fingerprint remains active.

title: [KAIZEN] Confirm issue-lock anomalies before escalating platform health incidents
description:
## Problem

[VITA-218](/VITA/issues/VITA-218) was opened with both a GitHub PAT failure and three issue-lock anomalies, but live triage showed the lock anomalies had already self-cleared before investigation began.

## Proposed Fix

- Add a confirmation pass or short recheck window before the platform heartbeat opens an escalation for issue-lock anomalies.
- Preserve escalation for persistent lock corruption, but suppress incidents that disappear on immediate revalidation.
- Keep the incident artifact trail so transient anomalies are still observable without creating stale operational tickets.

## Acceptance

- Platform heartbeat only escalates issue-lock anomalies after a confirming recheck.
- Tests cover both persistent and transient lock-anomaly paths.
- Existing GitHub/token degradations still escalate immediately.

title: [KAIZEN] Add safe rollout + detection for recurring stale checkout-pin regressions
description:
## Problem

The stale checkout-pin regression is recurring in the live service even after `/app` source patches were prepared. In the current queue, [VITA-188](/VITA/issues/VITA-188) is live with `status=in_progress`, `checkoutRunId=null`, and a pinned `executionRunId`, and even the fresh FE escalation [VITA-197](/VITA/issues/VITA-197) hit the same first-checkout conflict shape.

## Goal

1. Add a platform-safe rollout procedure for lock-lifecycle fixes so validated `/app` patches are not left unapplied during active multi-agent work.
2. Add automated detection that flags `in_progress` issues with `checkoutRunId=null` or self-owned `todo` issues with stale `executionRunId` before they require manual heartbeat inspection.
3. Document operator criteria for when a live Paperclip restart is safe versus when to defer until active runs drain.

title: [CHARTER_CONFLICT] PA Capabilities hardcodes 6-agent roster — Charter §2 defines roster as DYNAMIC
description:
Filed per the Charter Preamble deployed to my own Capabilities on 2026-04-17 (see top of Configuration → Capabilities). The preamble flagged this conflict as the "known charter-conflict to surface on first heartbeat."



The conflict:



PA Capabilities contains a block titled "PRINCIPAL ARCHITECT — 7-AGENT OVERSIGHT" whose body hardcodes "You oversee 6 agents (BB, FE, HR, BS, OC, DP)". (The title and body already disagree — title says 7, body says 6.) PA\_CHARTER.md Section 2 (Role and Evolution) explicitly defines the agent roster as DYNAMIC: PA sizes the team to the workload and may propose hires and fires. The hard number is a snapshot, not a cap.



Why this matters:



1. Instruction drift. If PA internalises "6 agents" as a limit, PA will not propose new hires when workload warrants one. That violates Job 4 (Adaptation Engine) in Charter §5.
2. 2\. Amendment path bypass. The charter says roster changes go through board approval (§11). A hardcoded number in Capabilities content creates a silent override — the count can be edited without a board decision.
3. 3\. Self-inconsistency. "7-AGENT OVERSIGHT" (current roster incl. DPM) vs. body "6 agents (BB, FE, HR, BS, OC, DP)" is internally inconsistent: DPM and DP are the same agent, HR is not in the body list. Block is stale.

Proposed resolution:



Rewrite the "7-AGENT OVERSIGHT" block on next Monday synthesis (2026-04-20):



* Remove hard numbers. Replace "6 agents" / "7-agent" with "the current agent roster".
* \- Single source of truth for the list: the /agents/ directory on Paperclip (queried live each heartbeat), not frozen text.
* \- Keep the oversight responsibilities language (reporting, coordination, budget allocation); just decouple it from a specific count.
* \- Add a pointer: "Roster size and composition are governed by Charter §2; propose changes via the quarterly amendment cycle (§11)."

Non-controversial — removes a stale hardcoded constraint. No board approval needed for the Capabilities edit itself (aligns PA with the already-approved charter; the board approved §2 as dynamic).



Action:

* Self-assigned. PA rewrites the block as part of next Monday's synthesis heartbeat.
* \- Output: updated Capabilities text + one-line \[CHARTER] closure issue.
* \- Timeline: 2026-04-20 Monday.

Severity: LOW. No active harm today — PA knows the charter overrides and this issue documents that knowledge. Cleanup so future PA instances don't re-derive.



Charter ref: PA\_CHARTER.md §2 (Role and Evolution), §5 Job 4 (Adaptation Engine), §11 (Authority Limits and Amendment).

title: [KAIZEN] Add retry-trend signal for intermittent LinkedIn transport failures
description:
## Problem

LinkedIn health is currently green, but recent platform-health artifacts show a repeatable cluster of successful-after-retry results. On 2026-04-06, four sweeps returned `200 OK` for LinkedIn only after one retry with `transportErrors=["fetch failed"]` before the pattern cleared in later sweeps.

## Why This Matters

- This is not a token-renewal incident today, so current health remains green.
- Repeated retry-only success can still be an early warning for network instability, upstream intermittency, or edge throttling.
- The current sweep output preserves the raw retry data, but it does not elevate repeated retry trends into an explicit operational signal.

## Goal

1. Add low-noise retry-trend signaling to the platform-health sweep for dependencies like LinkedIn.
2. Keep one-off successful retries non-paging while flagging short-window clusters.
3. Document how operators should interpret retry-only success versus auth failure.

## Success Criteria

- Platform-health output includes an explicit retry-trend signal when a dependency repeatedly succeeds only after retry within a bounded window.
- The signal does not escalate on isolated one-off retries.
- LinkedIn transport retry behavior has a documented interpretation path distinct from 401/403 token failures.

## Initial Research Artifact

- `reports/2026-04-06-linkedin-transport-retry-pattern.md`

## Sources Consulted

- `reports/2026-04-06-linkedin-transport-retry-pattern.md`
- `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-06/platform-health-sweep-024639Z.json`
- `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-06/platform-health-sweep-024710Z.json`
- `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-06/platform-health-sweep-024954Z.json`
- `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-06/platform-health-sweep-031647Z.json`
- `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-06/platform-health-sweep-032846Z.json`
- `reports/platform-health-sweep/2026-04-06/platform-health-sweep-034015Z.json`
